title: Resilient Reinforcement of CPSs against Sparse Sensor Attacks by Prior Information Decomposition
authors: Zheyi Zhao, Xuqiang Lei
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: The system resilience against sensor attacks is critical for secure state estimation of Cyber-Physical Systems (CPSs). Specifically, the upper limit on the number of compromised sensors that can be tolerated is a critical factor in determining the CPSs defensive performance. This paper proposes a method to enhance system resilience by leveraging double congruent information. A unique vector decomposition is introduced that equivalently transforms an initial state with dual congruent characteristics into two more sparse vectors. Based on this decomposition, an optimization model for secure state estimation is proposed, along with its feasible necessary and sufficient conditions. Theoretical analysis demonstrates that, under specific observability conditions, the proposed approach enables efficient state estimation through convex optimization. Numerical simulations on a diffusion process validate the effectiveness of dual congruent information in resilience enhancement. Results show significantly higher estimation success rates compared to traditional sparse optimization methods under identical attack conditions.

title: INTELLI-BDC: Intelligent Bidirectional DC—DC Converter for Optimized EV Battery Management
authors: J. Surendiran, S. Gomathi
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This paper introduces INTELLI-BDC, a smart bidirectional DC to DC converter that will be used to operate the advanced electric vehicle (EV) battery management systems. The architecture that is proposed will combine adaptive Fuzzy PID control, parameter optimization through Genetic Algorithm (GA) and fault-tolerant switching to achieve a high level of efficiency, fast transient response, and operational resilience. INTELLI-BDC is able to dynamically modulate PWM duty cycles based on load changes, battery state-of-charge and changes in temperature, unlike traditional converters, which operate on a fixed control set, and the converters also reduce conduction and switching losses by using a synchronous buck-boost topology with SiC MOSFETs. The optimal parameters controlled by the GA are the switching frequency, the duty cycle and the control gains in order to produce a low level of ripple, less power losses, and the shortest settling time possible. A machine-learning unit inside it estimates the malfunction of a MOSFET or sensor and recovers itself automatically by redundancy switching, so that it can continue to operate with minimal disruption. Simulations of MATLAB/Simulink and hardware checks on DSPs show 97.8 per cent conversion efficiency, 63 per cent transient overshoot, 66 per cent output ripple and less than 10 ms fault recovery. These findings confirm that INTELLI-BDC is a strong, efficient, and intelligent product to manage the next generation EV battery energy in a more intelligent way.

title: Algorithmic Midwifery: Benchmarking Machine Learning Models for Intelligent Fetal State Classification
authors: Niranjani K, Senthil K, Ayyapan G
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The fetal condition is of great significance in the prenatal care and minimizing the negative birth complications. This paper presents a baseline model, which is known as Algorithmic Midwifery, that implements classical machine learning algorithms to classify the intelligent fetus state through the Cardiotocography (CTG) signals. To classify fetal conditions into the normal, suspect, and pathological classes, three popular models, including K-Nearest Neighbours (KNN), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Random Forest (RF), were used. Very few preprocessing steps such as median-based imputation of missing values, scaling of the features, and label encoding were used so that the original data integrity was not lost. Grids of hyperparameters were optimized with grid search and 5-fold cross-validation, and the performance of the model was evaluated with the accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score and the analysis of the confusion matrix. SVM had the best performance and reported an overall accuracy of 92.4 which exhibited balanced accuracy-recall behavior. RF offered an important amount of feature importance scores, which are easier to interpret, whereas KNN was used as a simple and efficient baseline reference. Misclassifications that are present between Suspect and Pathological classifications underline the necessity to refine them further. All in all, this framework provides a solid classical-machine-learning baseline of fetal state classification and is a solid reference point of future deep learning methods, with practical implications of creating clinically-reliable decision-support systems in practice.

title: An LLM-Assisted Fuzz Testing Framework for Unmanned System Software
authors: Yupeng Zhang, Qing Wei, Shaoxun Liu, Yankun Xu, Haoyang Chen, Jinfeng Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: The booming application of unmanned system has led the software vulnerabilities of these systems becoming increasingly prominent. Fuzz testing is an effective approach to detecting software vulnerabilities particularly during the software development phase. However, the requirement for manually written fuzz drivers renders it a resource-intensive and expert-dependent task, which hampers the widespread application of fuzzing. To address these issues, we proposed an automated fuzz testing framework based on large language models (LLMs). First, we employ an intelligent code parsing module to circumvent the context length limitations of LLMs, enabling efficient processing of target project code. Second, we engineer a structured prompt template to guide LLMs in generating high-quality fuzz driver code. Subsequently, to mitigate the inherent propensity of LLMs to produce flawed code, we integrate a feedback-based correction mechanism. Finally, we have deployed an automated fuzzing platform and applied it to several typical software components for unmanned systems. The system performs notably well on relatively simple fuzz testing targets and alleviates the manual workload required for fuzz testing complex targets. Ablation experiments confirm that the feedback-based correction mechanism effectively enhances the performance of LLMs in fuzz driver generation. This framework provides a viable approach to automated fuzz testing and is expected to contribute to vulnerability analysis in unmanned systems.

title: MsGA: Gestational Age Estimation with Multi-plane Unified Measurements Driven by Anatomic Segmentation
authors: Mingjun Huang, Junbo Zhang, Wei Hu, Chao Sun, Xiantao Cai, Bo Du
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: An accurate estimation of gestational age is critical for prenatal care and clinical decision-making. Existing ultrasound-based gestational age estimation methods are limited by the insufficient information representation capacity of conventional medical segmentation models, noise interference in ultrasound images, and inter-observer variability in traditional geometry-based measurement methods. To address these challenges, we propose the MsGA model to estimate gestational age with multi-plane unified measurements driven by anatomic segmentation. In the anatomic segmentation stage, a lightweight and high-performance LGF-UNet module is proposed, which utilizes the Deep Patch Embedding module to expand the receptive field, the Local-Global Fusion Transformer block to enhance local-global feature fusion, and the Focusing Attention Bottleneck module to suppress ultrasound noise via an adaptive threshold. In the measurement stage, a Point Regression module is introduced to refine biometric landmark localization. Furthermore, we create a fully annotated ultrasound plane dataset for the estimation of gestational age across various gestational stages. Extensive experiments on the dataset have demonstrated the effectiveness of the whole model and each module. Our MsGA model is superior to existing models with fewer parameters and achieves state-of-the-art performance on the Gestational Age Estimation task.

title: RippleVision: Unobtrusive Gaze-Dependent Guidance via Directed Wave Motion in Virtual Reality
authors: Jérôme Kudnick, Daniel T. Mayer, Colin Groth, Bipul Mohanto, Ralf Dörner, Martin Weier
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: The inherent freedom of exploration in virtual reality poses a challenge for directing user attention toward relevant points or objects of interest, which are potentially located outside the user's field of view or are temporarily obscured. Accordingly, guidance must sustain users' perceptual focus while preserving the sense of presence. This paper presents RippleVision, a subtle gaze guidance technique using wave-like ripples that modulate brightness with inverted polarity between the eyes and appear only within a cone from the point of interest to the gaze location. A calibration study defined detectability and acceptability thresholds for the cues, followed by a comparative user study against four state-of-the-art techniques. Results show RippleVision achieves similar search times to clearly visible techniques while significantly reducing visual dominance. Moreover, its effectiveness scales with cue visibility, improving search performance with minimal impact on perceived visual obstruction and presence.

title: Photonic Sampler for mmWave Signals up to 70 GHz Using a 1-GHz MLL and a Silicon PIC
authors: Lucía Amaro-Losada, Cédric Bruynsteen, Shengpu Niu, Joris Van Kerrebrouck, Simone Cammarata, Kasper Van Gasse, Benjamin Rudin, Florian Emaury, Colm Browning, Nishant Singh, Xin Yin
abstract: Modern RF communications require the digitization of narrow bandwidth signals modulated on very high frequency carriers which can be a challenge requiring high-frequency oscillators and mixers for downconversion which can add jitter and distortion while being costly to implement. This work presents a photonic bandpass RF-sampling system using a silicon-photonic PIC and a 1-GHz 10.5-fs-jitter mode-locked laser where the MLL is modulated with mmWave complex modulated signals using a silicon photonics Mach Zehnder modulator and received using a low bandwidth photodiode. The usage of a MLL plus a low bandwidth PD demonstrates photonic downconversion of complex QAM signals at millimeter-wave frequencies up to 70 GHz, without the use of a mixer and local oscillator.

title: Compressor-Based Adaptive Approximate Multiplier with MFA and Clock Gating for Reduced Latency in Neural Network Applications
authors: SMD.Fayaz Basha, K. Riyazuddin, Vadla Ram Charan, Kalva Sucharitha, Pilli Vasantha Lakshmi, G.V Jeevan Kumar Reddy
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The field of approximate computing is rapidly evolving, particularly in neural network applications that can tolerate some error. I'm presenting a novel multiplier design in this letter. To reduce errors in each partial product column, it employs an algorithm that selects the best approximate compressors from both old and new sets. In order to speed up the MFA, I used pipelining instead of the typical full adders. A simple error-correcting module is built into the design to correct any errors resulting from this approximation. When I tested this 8-bit multiplier, it outperformed other recent designs and exact multipliers in terms of power consumption and power-delay product (PDP). Additionally, I tested it on image processing and neural network tasks, and it performed well, demonstrating its effectiveness and dependability.

title: IoT-Based Real-Time Gesture Talk System for Hand Sign Recognition in Speech and Hearing Impairments Using Cloud Computing
authors: Divyaa N, Boopathi Raja G, Devi Priya J, Dharani S K, Dharshan K
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: People with speech or hearing impairments often face challenges by a lack of realtime, cost effective and dependable systems for converting sign language to speech. Most existing methods of communicating with the non-speaking require either wearable devices with expensive hardware or processing done offline. All of these make communicating in real time in actual situations very difficult. It significantly limits the types of communication users can achieve therefore reducing how and if users will continue to be able to access these types of assistance and other related services. To solve these issues, the proposed system creates an intelligent assistance communications framework which interprets human hand motion, and translates them into meaningful audio messages with respect to hand motion detection and Hand Gesture Classification (HGC). The system includes the use of advanced computer vision techniques including MediaPipe and OpenCV. It is used to accurately identify and classify each of the 45 hand gestures; enhanced accuracy in the classification process will lead to capable and robust real time application under changing conditions of lighting, background, and hand orientation. It then sends the recognised hand gestures to an ESP32 microcontroller through the MQTT protocol, which will in turn trigger a DFPlayer Mini module to play the relevant pre-recorded voice response to each of the recognised hand gestures. The integration of cloud computing technology provides secure storage of data, as well as monitoring, analytic and continuous optimization, via remote accessibility. The quantitative evaluation results show that the gesture recognition average accuracy is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$95 \%-97 \%$</tex>. System latency from end to end is below 300 ms, therefore providing the framework with real-time assistive communication capabilities. The result shows that how AI based IoT architecture significantly enhances assistive technologies and improves the interaction among humans and machines.

title: TEE-Based Privacy-Preserving Arbitration for Dynamic Heterogeneous Redundancy in Industrial Control Systems
authors: Yukun Niu, Lei He, Xiaopeng Han, Yu Xie
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: Dynamic heterogeneous redundancy (DHR) enhances industrial control system security through multiple structurally diverse executors achieving consensus via arbitration, but exposes executor outputs to confidentiality attacks in cloud-edge architectures. Existing homomorphic encryption-based schemes achieve privacy protection at the cost of substantial computational overhead (31–99 ms arbitration latency), limiting applicability to time-critical scenarios, while adaptive approaches sacrifice privacy for efficiency. This paper presents a trusted execution environment (TEE) based architecture leveraging Intel SGX to eliminate the latency-privacy trade-off by processing outputs in plaintext within hardware-protected enclaves. We design a mutual authentication protocol based on SGX remote attestation and ECDH key exchange, and implement credibility-weighted arbitration algorithms supporting exact and inexact matching with autonomous edge-local scheduling. Experimental evaluation demonstrates 151× speedup compared to best-case homomorphic encryption schemes (0.21 ms vs. 31.86 ms) while maintaining strong privacy protection and unconditional arbitration autonomy, enabling deployment for sub-10 millisecond real-time industrial control loops.

title: Advanced Feature Engineering and Machine Learning for Simultaneous Cyber Attack Detection in Smart Digital Substations
authors: Aybüke Şenel, Mohammed Al-Hubaishi
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This paper introduces a machine learning solution for detecting simultaneous cyber attacks in smart digital substations, and we developed more than 50 features from GOOSE heartbeat and MAC address data. We evaluated 14 classifiers, including Random Forest, XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost, and deep learning models such as CNN, LSTM, and a hybrid CNN-LSTM, because we utilized the IEEE DataPort Smart Digital Substation Simultaneous Attack Detection Dataset. Ensemble methods achieved an accuracy of over 99% with F1-scores exceeding 0.98 for multi-class attack detection, and thus the hybrid CNN-LSTM achieved an accuracy of 99.72% and performed best in detecting replay, masquerade, and denial-of-service attacks. Feature importance analysis revealed that GOOSE heartbeat patterns, MAC source sequence patterns, and temporal anomalies were the most important features, therefore the proposed approach combines rich, protocol-aware feature engineering with powerful ensemble and deep learning models to provide a practical, real-time framework for securing critical infrastructure.

title: Blockchain-Enabled Decentralised Architecture for Biomedical Data Governance
authors: Devendra Singh, Shiv Ashish Dhondiyal, Ritik Bisht
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The increasing utilization of digital health systems that utilize EMR and EHR systems, computerized imaging, telemedicine, and Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) technologies, has, in turn, created a near-continuous stream of biomedical data. Although this data stream enables more effective and efficient clinical service utilization, and supports more data-driven clinical decisions, it has created challenges related to data governance, ease of integration, and other clinical service accessibility. Integrative data systems, such as EHR systems, must store sensitive biomedical data on centralized servers that, in turn, can easily be subject to unauthorized access and theft through a cyber attack on a singular, exposed access point. Further, biomedical data are residually dispersed in systems and/or data silos, such as hospitals, clinical laboratories, and diagnostic imaging centers, with no safeguards to protect from the loss of data verifiability, authenticity, and auditability. No blockchain technologies are currently integrated into existing data sharing systems. This study explicitly seeks to determine if incorporating data blocks into a persistent interventional data sharing structure mitigates the risk of loss of data integrity and access, as minimal access to data blocks is synchronously given to the entire peer network. This ongoing research seeks to determine if a hybrid blockchain-InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) structure, integrated with smart contract-based access control, enables confident computation of data, within the emergent health systems, as demonstrated in the compilation of validated datasets from the biomedical field.

title: PhysiKKT-Net: A KKT-based Neural Network with Guaranteed Compliance for Real-Time Power Flow Analysis
authors: Qingbin Jiang, Jianglin Feng, Zijun Wang, Haichuan Zhang, Siqi Meng, Ziyue Jia, Yujie Yang, Yang Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: The large-scale integration of renewable energy sources introduces unprecedented volatility and expands the attack surface for cyber-attacks, creating an urgent need for massive-scale, near real-time power flow simulations to safeguard grid security. To counter this and address the broader computational and trustworthiness challenges, this paper proposes the Physics-KKT Network (PhysiKKT-Net), a novel method that embeds linear physical constraints directly into the neural network architecture. PhysiKKT-Net accelerates power flow analysis while guaranteeing the physical consistency of its predictions, serving as a resilient tool for attack verification. Specifically, a softplus activation function enforces non-negativity, and a projection layer derived from the Karush-Kuhn-Tucker (KKT) conditions ensures strict compliance with Kirchhoff's laws. Validated in the Machine Learning for Physical Simulation Challenge, our method achieves significant acceleration while reducing violation rates of these fundamental physical laws to zero. By providing a trustworthy, physics-grounded baseline, PhysiKKT-Net empowers operators to rapidly distinguish between legitimate grid states and malicious data injections, providing a robust solution for secure grid operation, particularly under the out-of-distribution (OOD) conditions inherent to volatile, renewable-heavy power systems.

title: GraphDistill-TI: A Temporal-Structural-Aware Data Distillation Framework for Fine-tuning Threat Intelligence LLMs
authors: Kaihua Zheng, Jianan Huang, Jinsheng Sun, Weiwei Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: The application of Large Language Models (LLMs) to Cyber Threat Intelligence (TI) is promising in automating and scaling threat analysis. However, their performance is often constrained by the immense volume and complex dynamics of TI data. Current data distillation techniques for fine-tuning, which primarily focus on local sample quality, largely ignore the global structural relationships and temporal evolution inherent in threat campaigns. This oversight leads to inefficient data utilization with an insufficient grasp of the macro-context, particularly the time-sensitive nature of threats. In this context, we propose GraphDistill-TI, a novel data distillation framework, which preserves the temporal and structural integrity of TI data for efficient LLM fine-tuning. GraphDistill-TI first constructs a temporal-structural threat graph, integrating semantic associations, explicit timestamps, and key entity labels from raw intelligence reports. Then, it employs a Graph Attention Autoencoder (GAT-GAE) to learn structural representations for each report. These representations facilitate a K-Means clustering that divides the data into structurally coherent groups, and in turn, guide a novel weighted greedy selection algorithm. This algorithm strategically selects a data subset by optimizing intra-cluster representativeness, inter-cluster diversity, and fidelity to a predefined Expected Temporal Distribution (ETD). Experiments on a real-world dataset containing 16,493 TI reports demonstrate that fine-tuning GPT-2 on a 10% subset distilled by GraphDistill-TI not only surpasses a full-dataset baseline in a threat intelligence question-answering task but also outperforms random 10% sampling by approximately 6.0%. Furthermore, ablation studies reveal and quantify a critical trade-off between structural purity measured by clustering metrics and temporal fidelity measured by Kullback-Leibler divergence. By strategically prioritizing temporal fidelity, GraphDistill-TI enhances model performance on dynamic and time-sensitive tasks. Through delicately balancing the priorities, GraphDistill-TI establishes a new paradigm for domain-aware data distillation and paves the way for more efficient and context-aware LLMs in cybersecurity applications.

title: MindMorph: An Integrated Framework for Myers-Briggs Personality Classification Through Multi-Source Data Fusion and Advanced Ensemble Techniques
authors: Aishwarya A, Anisha Mehta Jain, Belde Sowjanya Laxmi, Suma L
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The Myers-Briggs Type Indicator framework serves as a prominent instrument for psychological profiling across various professional and academic contexts. Contemporary self-assessment methodologies face challenges in authentically representing behavioral manifestations while remaining vulnerable to systematic reporting biases. The advent of ubiquitous digital platforms has transformed textual discourse into a rich substrate for computational personality analysis. We introduce MindMorph, a novel classification architecture that harmonizes three heterogeneous information streams: structured scenario responses, unrestricted written narratives, and online social discourse. Our framework implements dimension-wise binary discrimination coupled with sophisticated ensemble algorithms to maximize inferential reliability. Through strategic amalgamation of diverse input channels with rigorous ensemble methodologies, our system demonstrates substantial advancement in computational personality profiling, registering an aggregate classification accuracy of 80.36 % across the complete MBTI dimensional space.

title: Minimizing Total Cost for Power Grid Energy Distribution
authors: Hitasha Singh, Nishant Kumar
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This document discusses the need for optimization of Power Grid in terms of the total cost and how we achieve the goal, what factors determine the total power generation and power transmission. The problem is formulated as a constrained data optimization model, closely related to optimal power flow, which could be further solved by various optimization techniques. The operation of the modern power grid faces the challenge of managing the growing demand for power, economical operation, and technical restrictions on the equipment used in the power grid. Through the application of these optimization techniques, it is possible for grid managers and analysts to analyze the impact of demand and boundary constraints on both the security of the system and the efficiency of the power system. Today, with the increasing demand of energy, it has become crucial to think about being energy efficient such that everyone and the future generations could benefit from utilizing power correctly.

title: Review of Cyber Security Challenges & Solutions in Cloud Networks
authors: Hiba A. Tarish, Mohammed Q. Mohammed
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: With the development of modern communications and the increasing spread of telecommunications and internet networks, as well as the exchange of information and data transfer, the urgent need arose to meet the requirements of cybersecurity against intrusive attacks and various breaches of cloud networks. Cloud networks serve as a communication area between users and all units of telecommunications networks, providing a suitable communication environment to facilitate data transfer. Therefore, achieving security in cloud networks has become one of the essential priorities to ensure the continuity of data flow and the privacy of information among users. In this study, a comprehensive scientific review will be conducted on the latest types of cyber attacks targeting cloud networks, along with their analysis and classification. This review also includes an overview of the most important and efficient techniques for detecting, repelling, analyzing, and classifying cyber-attacks according to the type of attacks and their targeting of cloud network layers. This review includes various statistics about the most prominent and recent articles that addressed the key methods and techniques for detecting and repelling attacks, along with the types of these breaches and their targeting levels. Finally, the intended benefit of this review is to generate a comprehensive idea about the information security requirements for cloud networks and to identify the most prominent types of cyber-attacks and the corresponding efficient and accurate detection and response techniques, in addition to understanding the targeted areas and layers in cloud networks.

title: The Use of Virtual Reality for Training Automated Control System Operators on Digital Twins
authors: Margarita V. Inozemtseva, Mikhail Y. Serebryakov
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: Traditional training for Industrial Control System operators, often based on 2D SCADA interfaces, is insufficient for preparing personnel for high-stakes emergency scenarios. These methods fail to replicate the necessary psychological immersion and stress, leading to critical human errors during real-world incidents. This paper analyzes the application of immersive Virtual Reality as an advanced Human-Machine Interface for operator training, coupled with a high-fidelity Digital Twin of the technological process. This «Operator-in-the-Loop» architecture enables the simulation to dynamically and accurately respond to operator actions in real-time. The evaluation focuses on metrics such as reaction time and error rates during simulated abnormal conditions. The analysis demonstrates that VR training on Digital Twins significantly enhances operator readiness. By creating a safe, high-fidelity environment that simulates both process physics and psychological stress, this method improves situational awareness, resulting in faster and more accurate operator responses to rare but critical events.

title: hls4ml: A Flexible, Open-Source Platform for Deep Learning Acceleration on Reconfigurable Hardware
authors: Jan-Frederik Schulte, Benjamin Ramhorst, Chang Sun, Jovan Mitrevski, Nicolò Ghielmetti, Enrico Lupi, Dimitrios Danopoulos, Vladimir Loncar, Javier Duarte, David Burnette, Lauri Laatu, Stylianos Tzelepis, Konstantinos Axiotis, Quentin Berthet, Haoyan Wang, Paul White, Suleyman Demirsoy, Marco Colombo, Thea Klaeboe Aarrestad, Sioni Summers, Maurizio Pierini, Giuseppe Di Guglielmo, Jennifer Ngadiuba, Javier Campos, Ben Hawks, Abhijith Gandrakota, Farah Fahim, Nhan Tran, George A. Constantinides, Zhiqiang Que, Wayne Luk, Alexander Tapper, Duc Hoang, Noah Paladino, Philip Harris, Bo-Cheng Lai, Manuel Valentin, Ryan Forelli, Seda Ogrenci, Lino Gerlach, Rian Brooks Flynn, Mia Liu, Daniel Diaz, Elham E Khoda, Melissa Quinnan, Russell Marroquin Solares, Santosh Parajuli, Mark S. Neubauer, Christian Herwig, Ho Fung Tsoi, Dylan Rankin, Shih-Chieh Hsu, Scott Hauck

title: A Neural Model for Contextual Biasing Score Learning and Filtering
authors: Wanting Huang, Weiran Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE Automatic Speech Recognition and Understanding Workshop (ASRU)
abstract: Contextual biasing improves automatic speech recognition (ASR) by integrating external knowledge, such as user-specific phrases or entities, during decoding. In this work, we use an attention-based biasing decoder to produce scores for candidate phrases based on acoustic information extracted by an ASR encoder, which can be used to filter out unlikely phrases and to calculate bonus for shallow-fusion biasing. We introduce a per-token discriminative objective that encourages higher scores for ground-truth phrases while suppressing distractors. Experiments on the Librispeech biasing benchmark show that our method effectively filters out majority of the candidate phrases, and significantly improves recognition accuracy under different biasing conditions when the scores are used in shallow fusion biasing. Our approach is modular and can be used with any ASR system, and the filtering mechanism can potentially boost performance of other biasing methods.

title: CASPER: A Large Scale Spontaneous Speech Dataset
authors: Cihan Xiao, Ruixing Liang, Xiangyu Zhang, Mehmet Emre Tiryaki, Veronica Bae, Lavanya Shankar, Rong Yang, Ethan Poon, Emmanuel Dupoux, Sanjeev Khudanpur, Leibny Paola Garcia Perera
publish: 2025 IEEE Automatic Speech Recognition and Understanding Workshop (ASRU)
abstract: The success of large language models has driven interest in developing similar speech processing capabilities. However, a key challenge is the scarcity of high-quality spontaneous speech data, as most existing datasets contain scripted dialogues. To address this, we present a novel pipeline for eliciting and recording natural dialogues and release our dataset with 100+ hours of spontaneous speech. Our approach fosters fluid, natural conversations while encouraging a diverse range of topics and interactive exchanges. Unlike traditional methods, it facilitates genuine interactions, providing a reproducible framework for future data collection. This paper introduces our dataset and methodology, laying the groundwork for addressing the shortage of spontaneous speech data. We plan to expand this dataset in future stages, offering a growing resource for the research community.

title: Architectural Design and Performance Analysis of a CUDA-Accelerated Framework for Collective Flocking Simulation
authors: Pujita, Sumedha Khosla, Saif Nalband
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: This paper presents the comparative performance evaluation of a CUDA-accelerated framework for simulating large-scale collective flocking dynamics. The model employs agent-based parallelization, where each agent is mapped to an independent GPU thread, applying separation, alignment, and cohesion rules concurrently. Optimization techniques, including coalesced memory access and shared memory utilization, achieved up to 300× speedup over sequential CPU baselines. The proposed architecture demonstrates near-linear scalability and serves as a foundation for real-time swarm simulations in robotics and virtual environments.

title: Real-space finite-element methods and algorithms for DFT calculations in the exascale era using projector augmented wave method
authors: Kartick Ramakrishnan, Phani Motamarri
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: Over the past six decades, Kohn-Sham density functional theory (DFT) has transformed materials research by providing a quantum mechanical framework for accurate prediction of a wide variety of ground-state properties. This predictive capability of DFT has made it ubiquitous across chemistry, condensed matter physics, materials science, and nanoscience, powering discoveries in catalysis, energy conversion, light-weight alloys, and electronic materials, while consuming 30-40% of the world’s high-performance computing resources today.y. Traditionally, DFT has been employed in high-throughput studies to establish structure–property relationships by screening large materials spaces, typically involving material systems with only a few hundred atoms. However, despite rapid advances in high-performance computing, state-of-the-art DFT methods are unable to fully harness emerging exascale architectures, thereby restricting simulations to length scales well below tens of nanometres, required to capture collective materials behaviour. Accessing these scales is crucial not only for understanding complex phenomena in realistic materials systems but also for generating rich, high-fidelity datasets that can drive machine-learning models towards predictive simulations across larger length and longer time scales. This work seeks to bridge this gap by developing novel real-space computational methodologies and scalable algorithms that are inherently suited to exascale architectures, enabling accurate, robust, and massively parallel ab initio simulations.

title: Mitigating VR Motion Sickness Through Multi-sensory Simulation of Wind Sensation (MSSWS): A Vestibular-Visual Synchronization Approach
authors: Yuan Yue, Chao Zhou, Tangjun Qu, Yan Hu, Junhao Wang, Juan Liu, Tianren Luo, Xiangxian Li, Yulong Bian
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Virtual Reality and 3D User Interfaces (VR)
abstract: Users are more likely to experience visually induced motion sickness (VIMS) during passive motion in virtual reality (VR), particularly in passive virtual driving scenarios. To solve this challenge, we propose a Multi-sensory Simulation of Wind Sensation (MSSWS) method to alleviate VIMS symptoms by enhancing the user’s sense of embodiment (SoE). The method utilizes low-fidelity airflow simulation to align vestibular perception with visual, auditory, and tactile cues. Then, we developed an interactive wind simulation helmet that can generate low-fidelity airflow along four directional axes around the user’s head, and implemented a passive virtual motorcycle riding environment with synchronized visual and auditory feedback matching the airflow patterns. A user experiment was conducted to systematically evaluate the effectiveness of MSSWS in enhancing SoE and reducing VIMS under various conditions, including helmet activation states, speed variations, and movement directions, with additional validation conducted in a Cave Automatic Virtual Environment (CAVE). Experimental results show that MSSWS significantly enhances sense of presence and SoE while substantially reducing VIMS during passive navigation. Notably, MSSWS shows significantly greater effects on VIMS in high-risk conditions characterized by rapid speed changes and multi-directional movement.

title: Unifying Model and Layer Fusion for Speech Foundation Models
authors: Yi-Jen Shih, David Harwath
publish: 2025 IEEE Automatic Speech Recognition and Understanding Workshop (ASRU)
abstract: Speech Foundation Models have gained significant attention recently. Prior works have shown that the fusion of representations from multiple layers of the same model or the fusion of multiple models can improve performance on downstream tasks. We unify these two fusion strategies by proposing an interface module that enables fusion across multiple upstream speech models while integrating information across their layers. We conduct extensive experiments on different self-supervised and supervised models across various speech tasks, including ASR and paralinguistic analysis, and demonstrate that our method outperforms prior fusion approaches. We further analyze its scalability concerning model size and count, highlighting the importance of selecting appropriate upstream models. Our results show that the proposed interface provides an additional performance boost when given a suitable upstream model selection, making it a promising approach for utilizing Speech Foundation Models.

title: Reconstructing Resources for Breakthrough Innovation: The Role of Divestiture–Acquisition Configurations Under Windows of Opportunity
authors: Huijie Sun, Yang Eric Zhou, Ziyu Zhou, Zhiying Liu
abstract: Resource reconstruction through divestiture and acquisition plays a vital role in driving breakthrough innovation. While previous studies have examined the effects of resource divestiture (RD) or resource acquisition (RA) separately, there is scant research exploring their combined impact on firms' innovation. Drawing on the resource-based view and attention-based view, this study theorizes and tests how strategic (in)congruence between RD and RA affects breakthrough innovation and investigates the contingent role of windows of opportunity (WOs). Using panel data from Chinese manufacturers and polynomial regression with response surface analysis, we find that incongruent configurations outperform congruent configurations in fostering breakthrough innovation. Furthermore, low-level congruence is preferable to high-level congruence. We also discover that RA-led incongruence generates more breakthrough innovation than RD-led incongruence. WOs can enhance the benefits of incongruence and low congruence while narrowing the innovation performance difference between RA-led and RD-led incongruent configurations. Our findings remain robust after thorough tests addressing potential endogeneity and alternative variable specifications. This study contributes to the literature on resource orchestration by highlighting the complex combined impacts of resource reconstruction strategies on breakthrough innovation. In addition, we provide practical guidance for adjusting RD–RA allocations in response to evolving environmental dynamics.

title: On the Adversarial Robustness of Efficient KANs
authors: Swetha Krishna Sriram, Aneesh Sreevallabh Chivukula, Anup Bera
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: Kolmogorov Arnold Networks (KANs) have emerged as next-generation machine learning architectures, valued for their interpretability and expressive functional representations. As vanilla KANs incur high computational cost, efficient variants with algorithmic and implementation-level speed-ups have been introduced to enable practical deployment. Despite their growing influence, the adversarial robustness of these new KAN architectures remains largely unexamined - a critical omission as these models move into reliability-sensitive applications. In this work, we present the first robustness benchmark for three efficient KAN variants, driven by a novel game-theoretical adversarial training framework powered by Quantum Particle Swarm Optimization (QPSO). We show that incorporating adversarially perturbed samples into training yields marked and consistent improvements in robustness across KAN architectures. Our framework additionally enables automated selection of the most robust KAN variant, providing a practical and scalable drop-in module for building interpretable and adversarially resilient KAN-based pipelines.

title: Accelerating Triton convolutions by exposing Shared Memory to the Programmer
authors: Shourya Goel, Ricky Dev, Pradeep Ramachandran
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: With each new generation of NVIDIA GPUs, advancements in memory bandwidth, tensor core throughput, and specialized hardware features necessitate significant redevelopment of CUDA kernels to maximize deep learning model performance. This process is resource-intensive and often results in brittle, hardware-specific code that is challenging to maintain. Triton, a Python-like domain-specific language (DSL), addresses these challenges by offering a block-based programming API that abstracts the complexities of CUDA kernel development.

title: Introduction to the 7th Workshop on Education for High Performance Computing (EduHiPC 2025)
authors: N/A

title: Mobility and Deadline-Aware RL-based Task Offloading Scheme in FC-IoT Networks
authors: Rajasekhar Dasari, Sanjeet Kumar Nayak
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: This paper proposed an online task offloading scheme for mobile IoT in fog/edge systems with tight deadlines and unreliable links. The proposed scheme provides a mobility and deadline-aware offloading method for fog-enabled IoT (FC-IoT) networks. It couples a lightweight reinforcement learning (RL) policy with constraints on latency, energy, and reliability. The action space includes local execution, single-fog offload, and staged offloading (current → next fog with a guard timer g). In simulation, the scheme achieves a deadline success rate of over 95% across various task arrival and mobility scenarios. It also improves the energy success trade-off relative to an always-replicate baseline.

title: Perceive, Adapt, and Interact: An Intelligent Virtual Agent for Education in Mixed Reality
authors: Naveed Ahmed, Zulaiha Afrah Sadakathullah Shaduly, Mohammed Lataifeh, Imad Afyouni
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Mixed Reality (MR) environments are reshaping education by fostering engagement and motivation. Within such environments, Intelligent Virtual Agents (IVAs) can serve as personalized guides, tutors, or collaborative partners. In this research, we present a novel IVA for education that perceives the learner's environment, adapts its guidance to their progress, and interacts with both the learner and the real world. Our multimodal IVA integrates speech capabilities powered by large language models (LLMs) with computer vision for dynamic scene understanding, allowing it to converse naturally with learners and provide adaptive support based on their progress. To evaluate our IVA, we implemented a Scratch programming scenario using the Magic Leap 2 headset. We conducted a user study with 24 university students (12 male, and 12 female) who completed the same tasks using three systems: basic AR cues, a chatbot with AR cues, and our IVA. Quantitative data were obtained using standardized questionnaires such as the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM), the User Engagement Scale (UES), and the System Usability Scale (SUS), alongside qualitative feedback. The results revealed that although the IVA system took a longer time compared to other systems, it fostered greater motivation and engagement, achieved the highest SUS score, and showed the strongest TAM correlations. Participants perceived IVA as the most valuable system, highlighting the potential of multimodal embodied agents as virtual tutors in MR.

title: Harmonizing Text-Guided Video Inpainting with Optical Flow Diffusion
authors: Bohai Gu, Song Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Text-guided video inpainting faces a unique challenge: while textual prompts enable semantic control, diffusion model often generate content that is spatially incompatible with the surrounding visual context, producing correct but incongruous results. This spatial disharmony, combined with the inherent temporal consistency requirements of video inpainting, creates a complex optimization landscape. We present Flow-based Efficient Diffusion (FloED), a framework that harmonizes text-guided generation with spatial-temporal coherence through optical flow guidance. Our key insight is that optical flow encodes the underlying scene dynamics, serving as a contextual bridge between semantic generation and environmental constraints. FloED employs a dual-branch architecture: a time-agnostic flow completion branch that captures scene-consistent motion patterns, and a main inpainting branch where multi-scale flow adapters inject this contextual information at different semantic levels. Besides, we introduce a flow-guided denoising acceleration that leverages motion information to reduce redundant computations during the multi-step diffusion process. Combined with our flow attention caching mechanism, FloED efficiently minimizes the computational overhead typically associated with optical flow integration. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FloED significantly improves spatial compatibility (4.9 dB PSNR gain) while maintaining superior efficiency, temporal stability and text alignment, establishing new state-of-the-art performance on both object removal and background restoration tasks.

title: Robotic Bin Packing via Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning_supp1-3678655.mp4
authors: Huixu Dong
abstract: The 3D bin packing problem has aroused enthusiastic research interest in recent years due to its wide range of real-world applications, such as logistics and warehousing. The packing sequence and placement pose (position and orientation) are the primary optimization objectives in bin packing, as they dramatically impact packing results and transportation costs. Existing methods either focus exclusively on sequential or placement decisions, or navigate a vast combined action space of both, generally struggling to reach global optimum. To bridge this research gap, we propose a novel approach that jointly optimizes both objectives. First, we introduce a packing configuration tree to represent the dynamic packing process. Second, we develop a hierarchical reinforcement learning framework which decomposes the problem into two tractable subtasks: a high-level manager network for sequence generation and a low-level worker network for placement determination. Third, we formulate a stepwise reward and train the framework using a Dueling Deep Q-Network. Finally, extensive experiments demonstrate the superiority of the proposed method, which achieves over 10% higher container space utilization compared to the advanced methods while maintaining robust generalization across varied container and box dimensions. Furthermore, real-world robotic packing experiments validate the practical applicability of the proposed method in industrial scenarios.

title: A Wideband True Time Delay Microstrip Line Five-Port Coupler With Double Ring Star Link
authors: Ayesha Naseem, Muhammad Uzair, Shehryar Niazi, Hanxiang Zhang, Bayaner Arigong
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: In this letter, a passive, matched, symmetrical five-port wideband coupler with true time delay (TTD) characteristic is presented. Unlike other five-port designs focusing on magnitude and phase balance, the proposed structure incorporates a compact delay equalizing element within a broadband microstrip ring-star core to achieve a constant TTD response, while preserving equal power division and ±120° phase relations. The theory analysis and simulation are carried out for the proposed design, and a simultaneous conductive and dielectric inks injecting 3-D printing technique is applied to fabricate the proposed five-port coupler with TTD response. The experiment results align with simulation and theory, and it achieves the expected constant TTD behavior across the 4–8 GHz range. The proposed TTD five-port coupler can be applied for wideband beam-squint reduction phased array design.

title: Performance Enhancement of the DMS Filter on SiC Substrate Using Intermediate Reflecting Grating
authors: Wei Guo, Pengliang Jia, Furong Li, Feihong Bao, Yedi Zhou, Qiaozhen Zhang, Jie Zou, Xianqi Lin
abstract: This paper presents a performance-enhanced, longitudinally coupled double mode surface acoustic wave (DMS) filter employing three interdigital transducers (3-IDT) on a 42° YX-LiTaO3 (LT)/SiO2/ $\alpha $ -Si/SiC multilayer substrate. By employing pitch modulation, the filter effectively suppresses bulk wave radiation, resulting in low loss and a flat passband. Furthermore, adding intermediate reflector grating between the 3-IDT of the DMS filter can significantly enhances out-of-band suppression, which is validated by comparison of finite element analysis (FEA) and experiment. The fabricated DMS filter with the intermediate reflector grating achieved a center frequency ( $f_{\mathrm {c}}$ ) of 1.83 GHz, a 3 dB fractional bandwidth (FBW) of 6.25%, and a minimum insertion loss (IL ${}_{\min }$ ) of merely 0.94 dB, closely agreeing with FEA results. In addition, the filter exhibited a power tolerance of 26.7 dBm, with lower and upper sideband temperature coefficient of frequency (TCF) values of +18.63ppm/°C and +7.23ppm/°C, respectively, indicating excellent temperature stability. Consequently, the proposed DMS filter with enhanced performance shows great potential for applications in radio frequency communication and radar systems.[2025-0226]

title: Robust Visual Servo Control with Visibility Constraints for High-Dynamic Landing of Quadrotors
authors: Xin Ding, Tao Jiang, Xiaojie Su, Chao Chen, Hao Liang, Hengrui Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: High-dynamic and high-precision landing of visual servoing quadrotors poses significant challenges, primarily attributed to the low-frequency feedback characteristics of visual sensors and the physical coupling between the field of view (FOV) and quadrotor attitude variations. This paper presents a composite nonlinear feedback control framework based on visual servoing (PBVS-CNF) for the autonomous landing of quadrotors on rapidly moving platforms. First, an adaptive interpolation-compensated extended Kalman filter (AICEKF) is designed to compensate the measurement delays and obtain high-frequency updates of visual positioning information. Subsequently, a composite nonlinear feed back performance-optimized controller based on a pixel repulsion function (PRF) is developed to maintain continuous target visibility during high-dynamic landing tasks, while enhancing the vehicle's response speed and terminal landing accuracy. A compensation function observer (CFO) is designed to estimate full-course landing uncertainties via multi-source state fusion, exhibiting higher accuracy and faster response speed than conventional observers. Finally, simulations and experiments validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Terminology Fragmentation of Infrastructure Calibration in Radio-Based Indoor Positioning: A Systematic Review
authors: Paul Schwarzbach
abstract: Radio-based indoor positioning systems rely on static reference points, such as anchors, access points, or base stations, whose positions must be known accurately. Methods that estimate these positions without manual surveying have been studied under inconsistent terminology across the indoor positioning literature, yet the research community has not converged on a standard name for this operation. Following the Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses (PRISMA) guidelines, this paper presents a systematic review of cross-technology terminology for autonomous anchor position estimation. The search covers four databases from 2015 to 2025, screening 461 deduplicated records and analyzing 36 included papers. The analysis reveals 20 distinct title-level terms for a single operation, with a 70% singleton rate. Body-level analysis identifies 137 unique variants. Ultra-wideband (UWB) accounts for 31 of 36 papers (86%), while Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) and 5G are absent entirely. Of the 36 papers, 17 (47%) provide explicit definitions, all describing the same core estimation task, yet terminology shows no consistent alignment with algorithmic approach. No convergence has occurred: the share of papers using self-calibration declined from 100% (2015–2017) to 22% (2022–2026) as competing terms proliferated. Full-text screening has confirmed a structural namespace collision: 78% of exclusions involved cooperative or collaborative terminology for mobile-based localization. Based on usage frequency, temporal precedence, and cross-technology applicability, this review recommends self-calibration as the canonical process term and anchor as the default entity prefix reflecting the UWB-dominant corpus. Technology-appropriate alternatives (e.g., access-point, beacon) are equally valid.

title: An estimation methodology for car wheel forces in fully general ride and handling driving conditions
authors: Leonardo Morelli, Riccardo Bartolozzi, Francesco Bucchi, Francesco Frendo, Marco Gabiccini, Riccardo Möller
abstract: Wheel forces represent fundamental information to assess and evaluate handling, safety, comfort, structural integrity and durability of vehicles. During driving, they can be directly measured with wheel force transducers (WFT). However, these sensors are expensive, costly and time consuming install and calibrate, and change the inertia properties of the unsprung masses. For these reasons, extensive research has been conducted in the literature to achieve a virtual measurement of the wheel forces, based on other available sensors on-board and on model-based observer algorithms. In the present work, a methodology for the estimation of the longitudinal, lateral and vertical wheel forces is presented. The novel aspects are in the wheel vertical force estimation, which is based on an extended 7 DOF ride model that includes also the transient roll and pitch dynamics. The methodology makes use of the well-established Extended Kalman Filter and needs signal inputs, which are typically available on modern cars. In addition to these signals, only the vertical unsprung mass acceleration is needed by the methodology. This method is combined with state-of-the-art modeling for the longitudinal and lateral wheel force estimation. The proposed methodology was validated on a high-fidelity virtual car model performing maneuvers on ISO C (rough road, low speed), ISO A (smooth road, high speed), and ideally flat tracks. Unlike data-driven approaches, this methodology is physics-based, requires no training phase, and ensures consistent performance under unseen conditions using standard onboard sensors. The results of different comparison metrics, such as Root Mean Square Error (RMSE), Normalized RMSE (NRMSE) and pseudo-damage, showed how the proposed methodology is able to estimate the wheel forces. In particular, the NRMSE was below 10% in most of the cases and especially below 3% for the vertical wheel forces.

title: A Multi-Scale Perimeter Control and Route Guidance System for Large-Scale Road Networks
authors: Xianyue Peng, Hao Wang, Shenyang Chen, H. Michael Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Perimeter control and route guidance are effective ways to reduce traffic congestion and improve traffic efficiency by controlling the spatial and temporal traffic distribution on the network. This paper presents a multi-scale joint perimeter control and route guidance (MSJC) framework for controlling traffic in large-scale networks. The network is first partitioned into several subnetworks (regions) with traffic in each region governed by its macroscopic fundamental diagram (MFD), which forms the macroscale network (upper level). Each subnetwork, comprised of actual road links and signalized intersections, forms the microscale network (lower level). At the upper level, a joint perimeter control and route guidance model solves the region-based inflow rate and hyper-path flows to control the accumulation of each region and thus maximize the throughput of each region. At the lower level, a perimeter control strategy integrated with a backpressure policy determines the optimal signal phases of the intersections at the regional boundary. At the same time, a route choice model for vehicles is constructed to meet hyper-path flows and ensure the intra-region homogeneity of traffic density. The case study results demonstrate that the proposed MSJC outperforms other benchmarks in regulating regional accumulation, thereby improving throughput.

title: Facial Expression Recognition for Chinese Elderly Using Edge and Semantic Features Dual Path Network With Two-Step Transfer Learning
authors: Keke Shi, Hongbin Chen, Keshu Cai, Jinhui Wu, Wei Wang, Jie Li, Bin Liu, Liangcheng Qu, Kuiying Yin, Pasquale Molinaro, Giancarlo Fortino, Jianqing Li, Wentao Xiang
abstract: Mild cognitive impairment (MCI), an early stage of Alzheimer's disease, often impairs cognitive abilities and emotional expression. However, accurate facial expression recognition (FER) in MCI patients remains challenging due to age-related facial textural interferences (i.e. wrinkles) and structural deformations (i.e. skin laxity). To address this issue, we propose ES-DPNet, a Dual-Path network that fuses Edge and Semantic features for robust FER in elderly subjects. Specifically, the Structure-preserved Edge Path leverages the Histogram of Oriented Gradients algorithm to preserve emotion-related facial structure and suppress local wrinkle interference; Meanwhile, the Region-aware Semantic Path employs large-kernel residual blocks with efficient channel attention to capture synergistic changes in facial regions and dynamically enhance discriminative cues. We have constructed the MCI-FED dataset, a high-confidence FER dataset with seven emotions from 25 Chinese MCI patients using a multi-model voting scheme to facilitate evaluation. We have introduced a two-step transfer learning strategy to adapt to age-related physiological and MCI-related pathological expression patterns. Experiments have shown that ES-DPNet demonstrates superior performance compared to existing deep learning methods, achieving 92.72%/85.22% accuracy and 92.77%/85.88% weighted precision on public youth and elderly datasets, 94.22% accuracy and 94.21% weighted precision in a subject-dependent validation on MCI-FED dataset, respectively. Our code is available at https://github.com/Codenewwer/feresdpnet.

title: Cross-Frequency Implicit Neural Representation with Self-Evolving Parameters
authors: Chang Yu, Yisi Luo, Kai Ye, Xile Zhao, Deyu Meng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Implicit neural representation (INR) has emerged as a powerful paradigm for visual data representation. However, classical INR methods represent data in the original space mixed with different frequency components, and several feature encoding parameters (e.g., the frequency parameter <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\omega$</tex-math></inline-formula> or the rank <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$R$</tex-math></inline-formula>) need manual configurations. In this work, we propose a self-evolving cross-frequency INR using the Haar wavelet transform (termed CF-INR), which decouples data into four frequency components and employs INRs in the wavelet space. CF-INR allows the characterization of different frequency components separately, thus enabling higher accuracy for data representation. To more precisely characterize cross-frequency components, we propose a cross-frequency tensor decomposition paradigm for CF-INR with self-evolving parameters, which automatically updates the rank parameter <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$R$</tex-math></inline-formula> and the frequency parameter <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\omega$</tex-math></inline-formula> for each frequency component through self-evolving optimization. This self-evolution paradigm eliminates the laborious manual tuning of these parameters, and learns a customized cross-frequency feature encoding configuration for each dataset. We evaluate CF-INR on a variety of visual data representation and inverse imaging problems, including image regression, inpainting, denoising, and cloud removal. Extensive experiments demonstrate that CF-INR outperforms state-of-the-art methods in each case.

title: Global Communications Newsletter
authors: Carlos A. Gutiérrez, Román Lara-Cueva, Kohei Shiomoto, Houbing Herbert Song
abstract: The recently established IEEE Guanajuato Section Communications and Vehicular Technology Joint Societies Chapter (IEEE COMSOC-VTS) served as a key promoter and main organizer of the Distinguished Lecturer Tour (DLT) of Prof. Gunes Karabulut Kurt (Polytechnique Montreal, Canada), IEEE Communications Society (COMSOC) Distinguished Lecturer. The tour was organized in collaboration with the IEEE Monterrey COMSOC Chapter and the IEEE Guatemala COMSOC Chapter, and included stops in San Luis Potosí, Mexico; Monterrey, Mexico; and Guatemala City, Guatemala.

title: A Contrastive Free Energy-Enhanced Transformer Framework for Efficient Reinforcement Learning
authors: Yue Pan, Jinlong Lei, Dechao Ran, Peng Yi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: To address the challenges of low training efficiency and unstable performance in multiagent reinforcement learning (MARL), this article proposes a novel efficient MARL algorithm called contrastive free energy-enhanced multiagent Transformer (CFMAT), which integrates upstream perception and downstream decision-making in a joint training framework. The upstream perception module consists of a representation encoder and a prediction model. Specifically, the representation encoder is first learned using contrastive learning to generate a unified and compressed representation of the joint observations. The prediction model is then designed to predict future observation representation sequences, which are subsequently utilized in the downstream decision-making module to enhance the policy network’s performance. To ensure the stability of the upstream module’s learning process, we propose a novel contrastive free energy loss inspired by active inference (AIF) theory. Additionally, the downstream decision-making module employs a Transformer-based encoder–decoder architecture. The representation encoder is further co-trained with the actor–critic network in the downstream learning process. By integrating the upstream perception and downstream decision-making modules into a joint framework, the training efficiency is improved by enabling efficient learning of the overall observation representation. We evaluate CFMAT on challenging multiagent benchmarks, where experimental results show that it significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in both training efficiency and stable performance across various scenarios.

title: Single-Source Cascaded Multilevel Inverter with Boosting and Cascade through Inductors for Magnetic Coupling Wireless Power Transfer Systems
authors: Xuejian Ge, Dingqiang Ren, Minghao Tang, Ling Li, Lei Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: This paper proposes a single-source cascaded multilevel inverter for magnetic coupling wireless power transfer (MC-WPT) systems, achieving boosting and cascading via inductors. An inductor is integrated into the first H-bridge to boost voltage, while subsequent bridges are connected through inductors rather than switches. This topology effectively suppresses current spikes of capacitors, doubles DC voltages, and enhances output stability and gain. Taking a five-level topology as an example, the operating principle, mathematical model, and parameter design are analyzed, as well as the charging and discharging processes of capacitors. Concurrently, the soft-switching operating states and power loss of the proposed inverter are calculated and evaluated, and its performance is validated through experiments. Experimental results confirm the inverter achieves a voltage amplitude gain of 4 and reduces reliance on capacitors. Furthermore, most switches achieve zero-voltage switching (ZVS), significantly minimizing switching loss. The proposed inverter maintains high efficiency across a 0–1 kW range, peaking at 93.3%, with a Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) as low as 17.06%. These results validate the feasibility and superiority of the proposed inverter for MC-WPT applications.

title: SLICE: An Efficient and Tuning-Free Keyframe Sampling Framework for Long-Form Video Understanding
authors: Sungjin Han, Thang Vu, Junyeong Kim
abstract: Efficiently distilling long videos into semantically significant keyframes is essential for effective Video Question Answering (VideoQA) using Large Multimodal Models (LMMs). However, existing sampling strategies suffer from hyperparameter brittleness, relying heavily on manual hyperparameter tuning or dataset-specific calibration. To address these limitations, we introduce SLICE (Semantic Length-Independent Content Extractor), a parameter-free framework. The core mechanism of SLICE introduces logarithmic scale-adaptive smoothing (σ = ln(N)) based on the video length N to suppress visual noise, and logically allocates a limited token budget through Information Density Partitioning. By partitioning videos based on actual Semantic Energy Mass rather than uniform time intervals, SLICE concentrates frames within complex event segments and aggressively omits static backgrounds, thereby securing both temporal diversity and semantic relevance. Experimental results demonstrate that SLICE significantly outperforms hyperparameter-sensitive state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods. Notably, on sparse event localization tasks, SLICE achieves an outstanding visual F1 score of up to 80.2% on HAYSTACK-LVBENCH and dramatically improves the temporal F1 score on HAYSTACK-EGO4D by nearly 3 times compared to previous baselines, all while reducing computational overhead. These findings prove that SLICE is a highly scalable, out-of-the-box solution for real-world LMM deployments.

title: A Hybrid Soft Asynchronous Actor-critic Framework for UAV-assisted IoV Task Offloading with Vehicle Trajectory Prediction
authors: Ying Yuan, Pai Zhu, Cong Wang, Guorui Li, Zhengmao Yao
abstract: With the rapid increase in computing demand for the Internet of Vehicles (IoV), the limited on-board computing resources have gradually become a bottleneck restricting the efficient processing of tasks. Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), with their high mobility and flexible deployment capabilities, have become an ideal platform for assisting IoV task offloading. However, the dynamic environment and the mobility of vehicles pose significant challenges for task offloading and resource allocation in UAV-assist IoV networks. This paper proposes a soft asynchronous actor-critic (SAAC) framework with vehicle trajectory prediction based on deep reinforcement learning (DRL) for UAV-assisted IoV task offloading. Firstly, in order to solve the problem of poor stability of traditional multi-agent deep reinforcement learning algorithms in dynamic environments, this paper combines the asynchronous training of multi-agent systems with the soft update and target network mechanism to improve the anti-interference and stability of the algorithm. Secondly, a module based on a recurrent neural network (RNN) integrated with a sliding window mechanism is proposed to address the uncertainty arising from vehicle mobility. This module enables precise prediction of vehicle trajectories, thereby facilitating forward-looking flight strategy planning for UAVs and reducing task transmission delays. Simulation results show that the proposed algorithm is effective, therefore this paper provides a new framework for the UAV-assisted IoV task offloading.

title: Two-Dimensional Model-Free Off-Policy Optimal Iterative Learning Control for Time-Varying Batch Systems
authors: Jianan Liu, Zike Zhou, Jinglin Huang, Wenjing Hong, Jia Shi
abstract: Although iterative learning control (ILC) has been widely used in batch processes, designing an optimal iterative learning control scheme for batch systems with unknown dynamics and time-varying parameters remains an open problem. In this paper, we propose a novel two-dimensional model-free off-policy optimal iterative learning control to achieve optimal control performance for linear time-varying batch systems. First, the one-dimensional state space is expanded to the two-dimensional state space by integrating time and batch information. Then, based on dynamic programming and a recursive algorithm, the framework of two-dimensional model-based optimal iterative learning control is established. Based on this framework, two-dimensional model-free optimal iterative learning control is further developed using model-free Q-learning reinforcement learning. The optimal iterative learning control policy is obtained through online off-policy iteration using historical and online operation data. Meanwhile, a rigorous convergence proof of the model-free optimal iterative learning control law is presented. Finally, the simulation results in the injection molding batch process demonstrate the proposed control scheme's effectiveness, feasibility, and significant improvement in control performance.
