title: An Embedded Tinyml Framework for Real-Time Anomaly Detection in Bluetooth Low Energy Wearable Devices
authors: Vignesh Kumar M, Sathis Kumar N R, Mariyam Jasmine S A, Pathma Sri S, Reshma E, Pritika R
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The behavioral properties have been found to be more efficient in detecting Bluetooth intrusion compared to the traditional protocol-signature-based approaches. To record such behaviors, controlled Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) attack scenarios such as advertisement flooding, repeated connections, GATT write abuse, and pairing request flooding were systematically generated and a dataset of about 29,000 BLE communication samples was obtained under both benign and adversarial conditions. An efficient machine-learning model was then trained using this data and tailored to be used on resource-limited hardware. Due to the constraints of the IoT devices, TensorFlow Lite on Microcontrollers was used and INT8 post-training quantization was implemented to decrease the size of the model without affecting real-time performance. The model was applied to ESP32-class microcontrollers and evaluated for the accuracy of detection, inference latency, memory footprint, and power usage. To increase the reliability and security of the systems further, firmware signing was introduced to eliminate any unauthorized changes and encrypted logging was also implemented to protect sensitive information.

title: Environmental Deviation Detection Using Intelligent Agents
authors: Akmal M. Khassanov
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: Detecting and localizing environmental deviations, such as air pollution sources, presents significant challenges in large-scale monitoring systems. Traditional centralized approaches lack the scalability and robustness required for real-time detection in complex environments. This paper presents a multi-agent algorithm for autonomous detection and localization of pollution sources using a distributed robotic complex. The system comprises a group of intelligent agents that operate collaboratively based on a multiagent approach, enabling decentralized decision-making and enhanced adaptability. The proposed algorithm was implemented and validated through simulation experiments on a custom-developed software platform. Results demonstrate effective localization of single pollution sources under various territorial configurations and operational conditions.

title: SLFGR StegoGAN: Self-Adaptive Local Fusion and Global Recovery StegoGAN for Few-shot Coverless Image Steganography
authors: Daqiu Li, Miao He, Lv Shi, Haihua Gu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: Existing coverless image steganography (CIS) works have not considered the scarcity of trainable samples in special classified scenarios, which leads to failure in ensuring the quality of stego images after steganography, which seriously affects steganographic security. To address this issue, this paper proposes a general few-shot coverless steganography method-SLFGR StegoGAN. Firstly, in the steganography phase, the method hides the secret information features in the secret image through a self-adaptive local feature fusion module to generate encrypted images, protecting important secret details in the secret image. Secondly, in the recovery phase, a global consistency reconstructor is designed based on multi-scale grouped dilated convolution and linear attention to enhance the recovery of key secret features and significantly improve the quality of reconstructed secret images. Finally, experimental results show that the proposed method performs better than the baseline methods on multiple datasets, with significant improvements in the quality of the stego images and the reconstructed secret images. This method can provide certain ideas for the covert transmission of secret information in few-shot scenarios.

title: Machine Learning and Deep Learning in Neuroimaging: Review of Brain Connectivity Analysis for Diagnosis and Prognosis
authors: Akshit Jay Kumar, Jimmy Singla
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Diagnosis of mental disorders like epilepsy, depression and Alzheimer's in an effective and “objective” manner is difficult. With the help of AI along with imaging methods like EEG and fMRI has helped researchers' study how different sections of our brain connect and work together. Recently Deep Learning models are becoming popular for brain connectivity and EEG data. They improve classification with the use of signal features and brain connectivity. Still limitation expresses through research methods differences, little transparency of deep networks and non-generalizable datasets. Future in this field can grow through explainable AI with common data standards and combination of multiple imaging types to build understandable and reliable diagnostic tools.

title: Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning Techniques for Android Malware Detection
authors: Veeresh K M, Bhukya Ramesh Naik
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Android malware detection faces challenges from evolving techniques like obfuscation and polymorphism, requiring more robust solutions. Current methods struggle to handle diverse malware variants effectively, impacting detection accuracy and real-time performance. This research compares ten machine learning-based methods, including Random Forest, LSTM, CNN, and SVM, using static and dynamic features. Methods were evaluated based on accuracy, F1score, and computational efficiency. Results show significant differences in performance, with Hybrid approaches offering better robustness and accuracy, achieving up to 98% accuracy and 0.95 F1-score. Future work will focus on optimizing real-time detection and enhancing scalability for large datasets.

title: Smart Accident Prediction in Fireworks Industry Using AI
authors: Manoah J, Anitha J, Immanuel Alex S, Stewart Kirubakaran S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Industrial safety has always been a critical concern, especially in high-risk environments such as fireworks manufacturing units, where even a minor spark can lead to catastrophic fire accidents. Traditional fire prevention methods rely heavily on manual monitoring and reactive systems, which are often too slow to prevent escalation. This project introduces a novel, intelligent, and real-time fire accident prediction system using a fusion of Artificial Intelligence (AI), Explainable AI (XAI), IoT sensor networks, and modern web technologies to mitigate the risk of fire hazards in industrial setups. The system leverages low-cost yet efficient IoT hardware components to collect live environmental data-such as temperature, humidity, gas levels, and motion. These parameters are continuously streamed to a backend server built with FastAPI, where a pretrained machine learning model performs predictions with over 97.5 % accuracy, classifying the input as either potential fire hazard." Furthermore, to ensure transparency and user trust, the model is integrated with SHAP (Shapley Additive exPlanations) to visually justify each prediction, making the system not only accurate but also explainable and interpretable. On the frontend, a modern Next.js application offers an intuitive dashboard to monitor real-time sensor data, prediction results, and SHAP visualizations. The system is backed by Supabase, which provides real-time database services, user authentication, and scalable storage-all within the constraints of a free-tier infrastructure. The entire stack is containerized using Docker, ensuring easy deployment and scalability across different platforms. This project bridges the gap between theoretical AI models and real-world industrial safety applications. It proves that cost-effective AI-driven solutions can be built using opensource technologies, significantly enhancing the response time, awareness, and preparedness in fire-prone environments. Future enhancements may include integration with emergency services (SMS/voice alerts), deployment across various hazardous industries (oil & gas, chemical,etc.), and the use of edge AI for decentralized real-time decision-making. In summary, this project represents a holistic, scalable, and intelligent fire prediction system capable of transforming industrial safety from a reactive to a proactive paradigm through the integration of AI, IoT, and explainable technologies.

title: An IoT-Enabled Refined Learning Framework for Sustainable Precision Fertilizer Management in Indian Agriculture
authors: Rambabu Kambhampati, Sankar P, Ravichand S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Effective use of fertilizer is crucial to improve crop productivity and sustain environment in Indian agriculture. This paper proposes an IoT based Refined Learning Framework to improve the fertilizer application effects via real-time filed monitoring and autonomous decision support. The developed ICT allows the integration of soil sensors, weather stations and growing crop within an IoT framework that will allow continuous capture of relevant agronomical parameters including data on soil water content and nutrients availability, pH, as well as microclimatic conditions. A sophisticated machine learning pipeline, which comprises of feature re-calibration, dynamic data filtering and ensemble forecasting models, is proposed to derive precise fertilizer applications at soil- and crop-dependent scales. Resource conservation is the central focus of this framework, with a goal to reduce nutrient loss and soil degradation and increase yield predictability and farmer decision-making support. Field trials in different agro-climatic zones of India showed the potential for improved fertilizer-use efficiency, cost benefits to farmer and eco-friendly sustainable precision farming through the system. This study also suggests the possibility of an IoT-driven learning environment to change ordinary farming environments into big-data and eco-friendly agricultural systems.

title: A Comprehensive Review of AI and Machine Learning–Based Diet Chart Recommendation Systems
authors: Smily Jasrotia, Anshiya, Jimmy Singla
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: With the modernization of the process of constantly increasing incidence of diet-related health complications, the necessity of individual preparation of a diet plan is now unquestionable. Nutrition is never a good solution because individuality is extremely different among the vast variety of hardware that includes lifestyle patterns, socioeconomic background, and various health goals. With this serious deficiency in mind, the proposed research is to create an advanced diet chart recommendation system. Such an innovative system will use the forces of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) to give very personalized dietary advice. The user will be given the freedom of typing their information in many ways, such as with images and texts. The multi-modal input feature is to make the user experience convenient and to be accessible. Besides, the system is made to be able to grow and develop. The model will also evolve, continually learning and retaining the user goals in real-time feedback mechanisms, which will successively advance its recommendations to become most accurately aligned to suit the preferences and changing health aspirations of the individual user. This adaptive learning has been made so that the system will become more effective and intuitive in the course of the use preparation and finally lead to improvement in health outcomes of its users.

title: Smart IoT-Driven Precision Agriculture: Advanced Feature Engineering and Ensemble Machine Learning for Intelligent Crop Classification
authors: Hamza Atouif, Mohammed Al-Hubaishi
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The combination of Internet of Things (IoT) sensor networks and machine learning (ML) is reshaping precision agriculture by enabling continuous monitoring and data-driven decision making. This paper proposes a practical end-to-end framework for multi-class crop classification from real-world IoT sensor data. Starting from 19 raw sensor measurements, we engineer 53 additional features that capture statistical patterns, geometric relationships, profile-shape characteristics, and domainspecific agronomic knowledge, yielding a total of 72 features. We evaluate eight ML algorithms under two training configurations (80:20 and 50:50 train-test splits). Among the evaluated models, XGBoost achieves the best overall performance, reaching 0.933 held-out accuracy on the 80:20 split and 0.925 on the 50:50 split. To account for strong class imbalance, we report imbalanceaware metrics and provide per-class and confusion-matrix analysis (using Random Forest) to characterize minority-class behavior. An ablation study over feature groups, carried out via stratified 5-fold cross-validation on the training data, suggests that the 19 original features already form a very strong baseline and that the full 72-feature representation attains comparable cross-validation accuracy while improving interpretability and supporting compact, deployment-friendly models. Compared with recent IoT-based smart agriculture studies that rely on similar non-image sensor data, our framework attains competitive accuracy on tabular IoT benchmarks while maintaining low training time and good interpretability, making it suitable for deployment on resourceconstrained edge devices. The proposed system supports real-time crop monitoring and resource optimization, contributing to the broader vision of Agriculture 4.0.

title: S3-FIFO Cache for LSM-based Key-value Stores
authors: Ivan Khramov, Timofey Komarov, Aleksandr Aladin, Ekaterina Balashova
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: LSM-trees are widely used in large-scale storage systems due to high write performance. The downside of LSM-trees is poor read performance. In order to solve this problem production-ready key-value stores provide efficient caching components to keep frequently accessed pieces of data in RAM. In this paper we discuss the principles of key-value stores based on LSM-trees, analyze RocksDB database as a real-world example of such a store and present a project aimed to implement RocksDB cache based on S3-FIFO eviction algorithm. We also provide performance analysis of the developed solution including cache hit ratio and throughput measurements.

title: Machine Learning and Deep Learning for Brain Tumor Detection
authors: Nahida Karaja, Nataly A. Zhukova, Yasser Nizamli, Kinda Mreish, Shaimaa Zakzouk
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: This article examines recent studies on the use of deep learning (DL) and machine learning (ML) in the diagnosis of brain tumors. It demonstrates how these technologies transform detection by outperforming conventional methods in terms of accuracy, speed, and automation. The paper concludes by discussing important issues like the lack of data and the interpretability of models and making recommendations for future lines of inquiry to further the area.

title: Sensory Integrate-and-Fire Neuron Model with Volatile Memristor Emulator
authors: Vasiliy Pchelko, Elizaveta Petukhova, Anastasia Kalinina, Kirill Samsonik, Alexander Mikhailov, Karina Kurtova
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: Third-generation spiking neural networks (SNNs) require biologically relevant methods for encoding real-world sensory information into temporal spike trains. To address this problem, sensory neuron models are being developed. One of the simplest and efficient models is a leaky integrate-and-fire (LIF) neuron, consisting of only a few elements, including a volatile memristor. However, prototyping such systems faces the challenge of a lack of readily available volatile memristors, necessitating the development of their emulators. In this paper, we present a simple circuit model of a volatile memristor emulator feasible for various spiking neurons. By numerical simulation, we characterize the spiking dynamics of LIF neuron with the proposed emulator, which depend on values of the resistive and capacitive elements. These computational results are confirmed by SPICE simulation of the proposed electrical circuit.

title: A CNN Framework with Grad-CAM Visualizations for Explainable Lung Cancer Diagnosis
authors: T G Manjunath, Shilpa Patil, Nikhitha B, Pavana Y G, Priyanka C, Varshitha M
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This research presents an understandable deep learning structure for preset lung cancer diagnosis using CT (Computed Tomography) scan images. A customized Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model is designed to categorize lung metaphors into three categories: benign, malignant, and normal cases. To ensure clinical transparency and trust, the system integrates Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM), which generates visual heatmaps to highlight the critical regions contributing to the model's decisions. The proposed model is developed using TensorFlow and Keras frameworks and fully deployed through a Streamlit-based interface to facilitate real-time and user-friendly diagnostic support. Extensive experiments conducted on the publicly available IQOTHNCCD Lung Cancer Dataset resulted in a classification accuracy of up to 95%, showcasing strong diagnostic performance along with explainability. This approach improves clinical validation and supports radiologists in accurate and faster decision-making for lung cancer detection. Additionally, the solution reduces manual dependency by ensuring early screening, which is critical for improving patient survival rates. The interpretability-driven design strengthens trust in AI-based diagnosis and encourages wider integration in healthcare environments.

title: Shadow Economy Detector Via Urban Activity Ml Analysis
authors: S. Raja, R. Manoj, D. Ragavan, A. P. Janani
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The shadow economy, encompassing hidden, unregulated, and informal economic activities, constitutes one of the major challenges for both regional and global contexts due to public revenues reduction, formal market distortion, and hindering sustainable development. Conventional measurement approaches-e.g., MIMIC models and currency-demand methodologies-are increasingly inadequate due to high latency, spatial resolution typically at the national level, and serious limitations concerning the underlying assumptions that are contested, and thus, unsuitable for targeted urban policy. This paper proposes a novel space-time anomaly detection framework for real-time, high-resolution estimation and mapping of the shadow economy. The approach fuses heterogeneous, high-frequency city-scale datasets-for anonymized movement patterns, digital transaction logs in aggregate, and commercial energy consumption-serving as a proxy for all economic activity. A Spatial-Temporal Graph Neural Network (ST-GNN) is trained for capturing the complex non-linear relationship between the activity proxies and the ground-truth economic indicators-e.g., tax registries, sectoral GDP. The Shadow Economy Index (SEI) is operationalized as a persistent statistically significant anomaly score, capturing the mismatch between model-predicted activity and observed official data. A privacy-by-design paradigm is followed by using aggregation and differential privacy. Preliminary findings indicate that the model can identify informal economic hotspots and generate a map with an estimated 85 % F1-score when compared against external validation proxies such as localized informal employment surveys. The framework provides a scalable policymaker dashboard, supporting the transition from static estimation to dynamic tracking and analysis. This highresolution tool enables selective interventions, such as formalization support programs, equitable tax policies, and comprehensive urban planning.

title: Slot Loaded Modified Circular Patch Antenna with High Gain for Modern Wireless Applications
authors: Mohan C, Madhubala B, Rethamya K
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The paper presents a high-performance circular microstrip patch antenna with Annular ring cuts on both sides. This design aims to improve current distribution and radiation behavior for modern wireless applications. The antenna is made on an FR-4 substrate with overall dimensions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0} \boldsymbol{\times} \mathbf{6 0} \boldsymbol{\times} \mathbf{1. 6} \text{mm}^{3}$</tex>, which makes it practical and cost-effective. It operates specifically in the 2.4 GHz ISM band and supports popular wireless technologies like Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and other short-range communication systems. The annular ring modifications adjust the resonant path and result of return loss of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{- 1 6. 4 7 ~ d B}$</tex>. This confirms excellent impedance matching at the target frequency. The antenna achieves a gain of 3.69 dB and shows a radiation efficiency of 87%, demonstrating strong performance for indoor and portable device use. Due to its simple geometry, stable radiation characteristics, and reliable operation within the ISM band, this design provides a solid platform for compact wireless modules, sensor networks, and next-generation smart communication devices.

title: Vizing’s Theorem in Near-Linear Time
authors: Sepehr Assadi, Soheil Behnezhad, Sayan Bhattacharya, Martin Costa, Shay Solomon, Tianyi Zhang
abstract: None

title: Research on Fault Diagnosis Method Based on ERPM and the Sparse Dictionary Learning with Label Constrain Associated Fisher Criterion
authors: Xihui Chen, Shiang Cheng, Qingyu Cai, Weiheng Zhao, Fengtao Wang, Kun Ding, Jingwei Zhang
abstract: The fault feature of mechanical transmission has the characteristics with weak and concealment, and fault signal is disturbed by the coupling of other components and working environment. A fault diagnosis method based on the enhanced relative position matrix (ERPM) and the sparse dictionary learning with label constrain associated Fisher criterion is proposed. ERPM is built by combining variational mode decomposition with relative position matrix to reduce noise interference and enhance two-dimensional features. The label constraint is added into dictionary learning that makes the signals of same types have similar coding coefficients, and the intra-class similarity and inter-class difference of atomic class are strengthened by Fisher penalty term. The discriminating ability of dictionary and coefficients are improved by updating two constraint terms alternately. The experimental results indicate that the proposed method excels in sparse representation and atomic cluster formation, enabling precise fault diagnosis within transmission systems.

title: RadioDiff-Inverse: Diffusion Enhanced Bayesian Inverse Estimation for ISAC Radio Map Construction
authors: Xiucheng Wang, Zhongsheng Fang, Nan Cheng, Ruijin Sun, Haibo Zhou, Zhou Su, Zan Li, Xuemin Shen
abstract: Radio maps (RMs) are essential for environment-aware communication and sensing, providing location-specific wireless channel information. Existing RM construction methods often rely on precise environmental data and base station (BS) locations, which are not always available in dynamic or privacy-sensitive environments. While sparse measurement techniques reduce data collection, the impact of noise in sparse data on RM accuracy is not well understood. This paper addresses these challenges by formulating RM construction as a Bayesian inverse problem under coarse environmental knowledge and noisy sparse measurements. Although maximum a posteriori (MAP) filtering offers an optimal solution, it requires a precise prior distribution of the RM, which is typically unavailable. To solve this, we propose RadioDiff-Inverse, a diffusion-enhanced Bayesian inverse estimation framework that uses an unconditional generative diffusion model to learn the RM prior. This approach not only reconstructs the spatial distribution of wireless channel features but also enables environmental building outlines perception, just relying on pathloss, through integrated sensing and communication (ISAC). The proposed method operates on routine communication measurements, without new waveforms, specialized feedback, or protocol changes, thereby enabling a plug-and-play ISAC capability. Remarkably, RadioDiff-Inverse is training-free, leveraging a pre-trained model from Imagenet without task-specific fine-tuning, which significantly reduces the training cost of using a generative large model in wireless networks. Experimental results demonstrate that RadioDiff-Inverse achieves state-of-the-art performance in accuracy of RM construction and environmental reconstruction, and robustness against noisy sparse sampling.

title: Robust Transducer-Reflector Distance Control in Acoustic Levitation
authors: Jan Helge Dörsam, Sven Suppelt, Alexander A. Altmann, Jun Liu, Chuanchao Xu, Christoph Haugwitz, Tomislav Maric, Dieter Bothe, Mario Kupnik
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Ultrasonics, Ferroelectrics, and Frequency Control
abstract: Acoustic levitation enables contactless manipulation for applications in containerless processing in chemistry and materials science. Single-transducer levitators, consisting of an ultrasonic transducer and a reflector, offer a minimal hardware configuration but require precise control of the transducer–reflector distance H. We propose a distance stabilization system based on a motorized linear stage and a precision balance that measures the acoustic radiation force (ARF) as a time-averaged, surface-integrated force magnitude on the reflector. Because ARF versus H exhibits extremely narrow resonance peaks, micrometer-scale deviations markedly reduce force and destabilize levitation. In practice, high-power transducers are actively kept at resonance using resonance frequency tracking (RFT). As the tracked frequency changes, the wavelength changes as well and the optimal distance H shifts. We introduce a two-stage control strategy in order to robustly stabilize H. First, a physics-informed, one-sided adaptive hill climb algorithm locates the cavity resonance. Second, a physics-informed extremum-seeking controller maintains the experimentally determined resonance distance despite drift and disturbances, using only the absolute ARF feedback. Long-term validation at node index n=5 confirmed that ARF remained within the 95% tolerance bound for two hours, ensuring stable levitation. Performance was limited by the balance, mainly due to its internal averaging time. This occasionally caused convergence to local maxima or temporary loss of track during object insertion. The strategy presented enables robust, long-duration operation of acoustic levitators.

title: Toward Unified Standards for Open RTL-to-GDSII Design Flows: Insights From the Open RTL-to-GDSII Flow Standards Workshop (ORFSW 2025) [Standards Corner]
authors: Hossam O. Ahmed, Ahmed Madian, Boon Chong Ang, Yongfu Li
abstract: The rapid expansion of open-source Electronic Design Automation (EDA) has created unprecedented opportunities for accessible, transparent, and collaborative chip design. However, the growing diversity of tools, data formats, and methodologies has also highlighted the urgent need for unified standards that ensure interoperability, scalability, and reproducibility across the complete RTL-to-GDSII design flow. The Open RTL-to-GDSII Flow Standards Workshop (ORFSW 2025) brings together academic researchers, industry leaders, open-source developers, and standards experts to explore emerging challenges and opportunities in establishing a cohesive framework for open silicon development. This workshop provides a platform for discussing standardization gaps in RTL design, synthesis, floorplanning, placement, and routing, timing closure, physical verification, and sign-off. Through invited talks, panel discussions, and community-driven dialogue, ORFSW 2025 aims to accelerate the creation of practical, consensus-based standards that enable reliable design portability, enhance toolchain interoperability, and support the long-term sustainability of the open hardware ecosystem. By fostering cross-domain collaboration, the workshop seeks to strengthen the global movement toward democratized chip design and further advance the IEEE CASS Standards Activities Subdivision’s mission to shape the future of open-source EDA and semiconductor innovation.

title: High-Sensitivity MMF-TCF-MMF Sensor Based on the Synergistic Effect of Au Film and AgNPs
authors: Fangzheng Wang, Yunhao Ma, Liyin Hu, Xingjun Yue, Xin Yan
abstract: In this work, a high-performance sensor based on a multimode fiber-thin core fiber-multimode fiber (MMF-TCF-MMF) structure is demonstrated. By using a continuous Au film and covalently anchored AgNPs on the TCF surface, the synergistic coupling between exciting surface plasmon resonance (SPR) and localized surface plasmon resonance (LSPR) is realized. Following a systematic optimization guided by COMSOL simulations and experimental validation, the refractive index (RI) sensitivity reached 2525.06 nm/RIU with a Figure of Merit (FOM) of 9.494 RIU<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>, and the temperature sensitivity reached -1.07 nm/°C. The system demonstrated excellent linearity, repeatability, and stability. The proposed sensor effectively merges cost-efficiency with robust precision, showing significant potential for biochemical, environmental, and industrial sensing applications.

title: SARCapsNet: A Capsule Network for High-Accuracy SAR Automatic Target Recognition Under Limited Training Sample Conditions
authors: Zhiyuan Liao, Chunquan Li, Junjie Zeng, Junzhi Yu, Yachao Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Most existing synthetic aperture radar (SAR) automatic target recognition (ATR) methods perform well with large-scale training data, but their performance significantly decreases in small-sample scenarios. Therefore, developing a SAR ATR method that can provide high accuracy and lightweight performance under different training sample sizes is a highly challenging problem. To address this problem, this paper pro poses a capsule network, named SARCapsNet, which consists of a hierarchical adaptive threshold capsule encoder (HATCE) and a reconstruction decoder. Particularly, HATCE is the most significant innovation of the proposed SARCapsNet. Specifically, in the proposed HATCE, an adaptive deformable threshold module (ADTM) is first developed by rationally fusing deformable convolution and soft threshold denoising techniques. This ADTM can adaptively capture the complex morphological features and key scattering distribution of SAR targets, while mitigating the impact of speckle noise. Second, different from ADTM, an adaptive thresholding module (ATM) is developed by reasonably integrating standard convolution with soft threshold denoising so that such ATM can further learn morphology-stable high-level features and enhance the model's ability to capture more robust and generalized structural features. Furthermore, a shuffle attention mechanism is incorporated into the HATCE to adaptively emphasize important features by capturing fine-grained, pixel level pairwise relationships and inter-channel dependencies, while suppressing irrelevant information. By ingeniously integrating ADTM, ATM, and shuffle attention mechanisms into HATCE, SARCapsNet can not only provide excellent recognition accuracy under different numbers of training samples but also exhibit lightweight characteristics. The experimental results show that SARCapsNet not only outperforms state-of-the-art models under both limited and full training sample conditions, but also maintains its lightweight characteristics. For instance, SARCapsNet achieves a recognition accuracy of 95.75% on the MSTAR dataset with only 10% of the training samples.

title: Machine Learning to Secure Wheels: A Survey of Misbehaviour Detection for the Next Generation of Connected and Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Aadil Hussain Lone, Ali A. Nasir, Muhammad Khurram Khan, Ali Alshaikhi, Mohamed Mohandes, Mahmoud M. Salim, Daniel B. Da Costa
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Connected and autonomous vehicles (CAVs) rely on 5G vehicle-to-everything (V2X) communications to enable safety critical and cooperative applications such as emergency braking, collective perception, platooning, and hazard warning. Public key infrastructure (PKI) ensures authentication and message integrity but is insufficient against insider attacks, data manipulation, and fault induced anomalies in highly dynamic vehicular environments. As a result, misbehaviour detection systems (MDSs), supported by machine learning (ML), have become an essential complement to cryptographic security. This survey presents a comprehensive and deployment-oriented review of ML-based MDSs for next-generation CAV ecosystems. Unlike existing surveys that focus mainly on attack-centric or algorithm-centric perspectives, this work explicitly considers both intentional misbehaviour, including Sybil attacks, message falsification, and denial of service, and unintentional misbehaviour arising from sensor faults, protocol deviations, and environmental interference. We introduce a novel five-dimensional classification of misbehaviour detection approaches, encompassing knowledge-driven, data-driven, trust and reputation-driven, context-aware and adaptive, and hybrid and fusion-based systems, organized around how evidential information is generated, validated, and fused. Furthermore, the survey proposes a structured taxonomy of ML-based MDSs that separates learning regimes, including supervised, unsupervised, and semi-supervised, and reinforcement learning, from deployment and adaptation mechanisms such as federated and transfer learning, reflecting the non-IID data, mobility, and privacy constraints of CAV networks. We examine representative V2X use cases to connect operational requirements with suitable detection strategies, and open challenges related to zero-day detection, adversarial robustness, real-time operation, and standardization are highlighted.

title: A Systematic Review of Takeover System Design in Highly Automated Driving
authors: Tianrui Liu, Tram Thi Minh Tran, Kristina Mah, Callum Parker, Anastasia Globa
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: As vehicle automation advances toward highly automated driving, takeover systems must address scenarios where users are entirely disengaged from the driving task. Although highly automated driving remains largely experimental, designing and researching robust takeover systems is essential for ensuring safe deployment, fostering user trust, and promoting system acceptance. Existing research has explored individual aspects of takeover design and interaction, but a comprehensive synthesis remains lacking. This paper presents a systematic review of 61 studies between 2015 and 2024, retrieved from the ACM Digital Library, Web of Science, ScienceDirect, and IEEE Xplore. The review is structured across three core dimensions: 1) takeover system design, including takeover budgets, modalities, and prompt information; 2) takeover scenarios, including driver characteristics, non-driving-related tasks, and traffic environments; and 3) takeover evaluation, including methods, measurement, and experimental apparatus. The analysis highlights inconsistencies in the definitions of automation levels and discusses their implications for comparability and validity in highly-automated driving research. It then identifies concrete research priorities: adaptive multimodal takeover systems, unified evaluation metrics, and ethical frameworks to guide human–system collaboration.

title: IEEE Foundation
authors: N/A

title: Detection of Out-of-Distribution Power Quality Disturbance using Dual-Channel Gramian Angular Field with Enhanced Mahalanobis Distance
authors: Yixiang Zhang, Huifang Wang, Bin Luo, Yuzhen Zheng, Mohammad Shahidehpour, Wentong Chen, Kaifeng Lin
abstract: Deep learning methods for the power quality disturbance (PQD) classification encounter significant deployment challenges in power system applications. With the increasing integration of renewable energy, electric vehicles, and other non-traditional loads, the out-of-distribution (OOD) data occurs frequently due to evolving power system conditions, which represents a critical yet underexplored challenge. In this study, the OOD problem in the PQD classification is formally defined, where models encounter the absence of disturbance patterns during training. Leveraging the unique characteristics of PQD data, a four-stage multimodal OOD detection method is developed for the OOD-driven framework to enhance PQD classification. The proposed method first transforms PQD waveforms into dual-channel Gramian Angular Field (GAF) maps, then extracts discriminative features with vision Transformers. Mahalanobis distance is employed for OOD scoring, and a multimodal auxiliary criterion is introduced to enhance the distinction between in-distribution (IND) and OOD. Comprehensive case studies are conducted using both simulated IEEE standard-compliant waveforms and practical PQD data. Results applied to the simulated PQD data demonstrate that the proposed method can achieve a 2.10% of false positive rate at 95% true positive recall (FPR95), outperforming all previous methods. Practical applications demonstrate a remarkable 9.64% improvement in the PQD classification accuracy compared to the unfiltered dataset containing OOD samples.

title: CON-FC: A Channel Occupancy Notification-Based Fair Coexistence Method for NR-U and WiFi
authors: Peiliang Zuo, Gang Gu, Yanyan Han, Xiangtian Zhao, Ronglei Hu
abstract: With the explosive growth in communication de mand for the fifth generation (5G) mobile communication system, research into access based on new radio in the unlicensed band (NR-U) has become key to improve network performance. Existing coexistence methods typically simulate NR-U as WiFi nodes and dynamically adjust their listen-before-talk (LBT) related parameters for coexistence. However, these approaches struggle to reliably manage channel occupancy, resulting in wasted channel time resources and limited coexistence perfor mance. In light of this, this paper proposes a channel occupancy notification-based fair coexistence (CON-FC) method for NR-U and WiFi, addressing the interference and fairness issues between the two types of networks. The method optimizes the channel utilization efficiency of the NR-U system through a reliable channel occupancy notification mechanism based on the Clear-to-Send (CTS) frame and Physical Layer Convergence Procedure (PLCP) header of valid WiFi communication frame, and the design of policies and algorithms for NR-U accesses, which reduces ineffective fallbacks andWiFi device conflicts. The results of the simulation test show that, compared to the traditional LBT method, CON-FC improves the fairness index by an average of 7.43% and increases overall throughput by 11.53%-71.82% in various network configurations. This method not only meets the fairness requirements of the 3rd Generation Partnership Project (3GPP) but also significantly enhances overall system perfor mance, providing a reliable solution for the efficient coexistence of NR-U and WiFi in the unlicensed spectrum.

title: Research, Analysis and Optimization of Welding Eddy Current Loss for AFPM Propulsion Machine with Yokeless and Segmented Armature
authors: Chen Wang, Yanbo Lu, Ya Li, Zhuoran Zhang, Hui Yang
abstract: The welding process is a crucial technological means for axial flux permanent magnet (AFPM) machines with yokeless and segmented armature (YASA) due to the distinctive modular stator structure. The eddy-current loops formed in the stator core due to the welding process result in severe loss concentration under the planar air gap. To address this challenge, this paper explores welding stator core eddy-current losses (WSCECL) on YASA AFPM machines. Firstly, the generation mechanism of the WSCECL caused by the welding seam (WS) is revealed, and an analytical model (AM) of the WSCECL is deduced with consideration of the skin effect. Then, the influences of key parameters, including laminated silicon steel (LSS) sheet thickness, welding radius, and welding location, on the WSCECL are investigated and optimized. Several different welding methods are proposed based on the existing level of welding process. The impacts of different welding processes on the losses of YASA AFPM machines are compared, demonstrating the superiority of the self-adhesive sheet process. Finally, two 30kW YASA AFPM machine prototypes with different stator core lamination processes are manufactured and tested. The experimental results confirm that the effectiveness and feasibility of the self-adhesive sheet process for WSCECL reduction and effective improvement.

title: Cover 4
authors: N/A

title: Risk-Aware AIoT-Based Fault Detection for Power Line Monitoring in Consumer Energy Systems
authors: Tianjing Wang, Sunhua Huang, Zhijun Zhang, Zuliang Huang, Zuqing Zheng, Zhao Yang Dong
abstract: Reliable power line monitoring is critical for consumer energy systems that increasingly depend on distributed sensors, smart terminals, and IoT-enabled edge devices. In practical deployments, fault detection performance is often affected by changing operating conditions, noisy measurements, and unreliable prediction confidence. This paper proposes a risk-aware AIoT-based fault detection framework that integrates multi-domain signal representation, uncertainty-aware inference, and confidence-adaptive decision making. High-frequency waveform signals are analyzed across time, frequency, and wavelet domains to capture complementary fault characteristics, and are adaptively fused to preserve localized and nonstationary patterns. Instead of producing deterministic outputs, a Dirichlet-based Prior Network is employed to jointly estimate fault probability and predictive uncertainty. These quantities are combined into a scalar risk score that enables a three-outcome decision strategy, allowing the system to issue alarms, defer uncertain cases, or identify normal operation. Experiments on a real-world power line dataset show that the proposed approach maintains stable detection performance and well-calibrated confidence under distribution shift. The results demonstrate that incorporating uncertainty-aware inference and risk-adaptive decision logic improves the reliability of AI-enabled monitoring systems, making the framework suitable for IoT-driven consumer energy applications.

title: Quantification and Correction of SMAP Soil Moisture Retrieval Biases Induced by Subpixel Moisture and Texture Heterogeneity over China
authors: Peilin Song, Xiaojun Li, Tianjie Zhao, Shengda Niu, Yanlong Sun, Liang H
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Surface soil moisture (SSM) plays a vital role in regulating terrestrial water and energy exchanges. However, SSM retrievals from passive microwave (PMW) sensors such as Soil Moisture Active Passive (SMAP) are theoretically biased by subpixel soil heterogeneity, particularly the combined variability of soil moisture and soil texture within coarse-resolution footprints. This study investigates and corrects these heterogeneity-induced biases in SMAP SSM products. Using a 1-km downscaled SSM dataset, we quantify how fine-scale soil moisture variability propagates through the nonlinear radiative transfer model (RTM) and induces systematic dry biases at the coarse scale. Specifically, the bias magnitude increases nonlinearly with the subpixel standard deviation of SSM, an indicator of soil moisture heterogeneity, and is further amplified by soil texture variability. Fitting this relationship with a quadratic function enables the construction of pixel-specific bias correction models. Validation against dense in situ measurements from automatic soil moisture stations in China shows that, in the best-case scenario, the correction reduces ubRMSE from 0.044 to 0.033 cm³/cm³ and improves correlation from 0.80 to 0.90. A nationwide assessment (2016–2023) reveals that relative bias is generally low (1–5%) but can exceed 15% in strongly heterogeneous regions and reach up to 50% under extreme conditions. Overall, the findings underscore the necessity of accounting for subpixel soil moisture and texture heterogeneity to enhance the accuracy of PMW-derived SSM products. The proposed correction approach is transferable to current and forthcoming microwave missions, including Soil Moisture and Ocean Salinity Mission (SMOS) and Copernicus Imaging Microwave Radiometer (CIMR).

title: Model-Free Predictive Current Control With Robust Reference for Grid-Connected Voltage Source Inverter With LCL Filter
authors: Zhuangzhuang Feng, Cungang Hu, Wenping Cao, Tao Rui, Zheng Yin
abstract: Model predictive current control often suffers from limited robustness due to its reliance on accurate model parameters of inductor-capacitor-inductor (LCL) filtered voltage source inverters (VSIs). To solve this problem, a model-free predictive current control with robust reference is proposed for LCL filtered VSIs. First, a triple-vector model-free prediction is proposed based on system variable gradients for reducing current ripple without requiring model parameters. Next, a two-step updating mechanism is established to refresh the system variable gradients, so as to enhance the accuracy of the system variable gradients and triple-vector model-free prediction. In addition, a robust reference is generated by combining sampled data and mathematical models, which mitigates the effects of parameter variations on reference accuracy. Finally, both simulation and experiment demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Security Cooperative Path-Tracking for Connected Vehicle Swarm Resilient to Dual-Channel Deception Attacks
authors: Zhenyu Chang, Guangdeng Zong, Wenhai Qi, Xudong Zhao, Xiang Liu
abstract: This paper investigates the security path-tracking control problem for the connected vehicle swarm with uncertain dynamics, specifically emphasizing its resilience against dual-channel deception attacks. To ensure the security and reliability of data transmission against such cyber threats, a novel attack compensation mechanism is proposed to eliminate the impact of deception attacks by securing data transmission against signal tampering in both sensor-controller and controller-actuator channels. We design a fuzzy state estimator to reconstruct uncompromised signals from tampered data, thereby constructing a security control strategy resilient to deception attacks. Considering practical network bandwidth constraints, a fuzzy adaptive dynamic event-triggered path-tracking control strategy is developed, which integrates the proposed attack compensation mechanism and state estimator to mitigate the impact of cyber attacks. Sufficient conditions are established to guarantee the security path-tracking performance. With the Lyapunov stability theory, it is proved that error systems are uniformly ultimately bounded, and the Zeno phenomenon can be effectively prevented. Simulation results validate the effectiveness of the theoretical approach.

title: Joint Multimodal Contrastive Learning for Robust Spoken Term Detection and Keyword Spotting
authors: Ramesh Gundluru, Shubham Gupta, K Sri Rama Murty
publish: 2025 IEEE Automatic Speech Recognition and Understanding Workshop (ASRU)
abstract: Acoustic Word Embeddings (AWEs) improve the efficiency of speech retrieval tasks such as Spoken Term Detection (STD) and Keyword Spotting (KWS). However, existing approaches suffer from limitations, including unimodal supervision, disjoint optimization of audio-audio and audio-text alignment, and the need for task-specific models. To address these shortcomings, we propose a joint multimodal contrastive learning framework that unifies both acoustic and cross-modal supervision in a shared embedding space. Our approach simultaneously optimizes: (i) audio-text contrastive learning, inspired by the CLAP loss, to align audio and text representations and (ii) audio-audio contrastive learning, via Deep Word Discrimination (DWD) loss, to enhance intra-class compactness and inter-class separation. The proposed method outperforms existing AWE baselines on word discrimination task while flexibly supporting both STD and KWS. To our knowledge, this is the first comprehensive approach of its kind.

title: Communication-Centric UALink and NVLink Comparison for Large Scale AI Training
authors: Sai Krishna Vemuri, Ajay Joshi, Vijay Kumar Motagi, Rohit Sindhu, Venkata Ravi Shankar Jonnalagadda, Amandeep Singh, Praveen Kumar Rukmangada, Sachin Shaw, Vishal Tanna
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: This paper presents a UALink-first comparative analysis of interconnects for large-scale AI training. UALink 1.0 is an open, vendor-neutral, rack-scale fabric that provides memory-semantic load/store, deterministic latency targets below one microsecond request-to-response and scaling up to 1024 accelerators per pod. We develop analytical models for latency and bandwidth-sensitive collectives common in transformer training and instantiate them under flat and hierarchical topologies. We compare UALink to NVIDIA’s NVLink generations 3–5, which represent the current proprietary state-of-the-art in per-GPU bandwidth and switch-based scaling. Results show that a flat UALink pod can reduce the step-time contribution of small-message collectives at scales of up to 1024 GPUs, while NVLink 5 maintains an advantage in bandwidth dominated regimes due to aggregate per-GPU bandwidth near 1.8 TB/s and large non-blocking pods. The figure-generation methodology directly maps tabulated parameters into closed-form equations, making assumptions explicit and reproducible.

title: CLIP Feature Selection Mechanism via Sparse Autoencoder for Generalized Deepfake Detection
authors: Lorenzo Berlincioni, Andrea Ciamarra, Pietro Pala, Roberto Caldelli, Alberto Del Bimbo
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Information Forensics and Security (WIFS)
abstract: Generating multimedia contents, particularly images, by using AI-based tool is becoming an everyday practice generally just for fair applications but always more for malevolent aims such as disinformation, defamation and blaming. Reliably detecting synthetic generated pictures is becoming crucial. However, new generative models are emerging at a much faster pace than the development of accurate deepfake detectors. According to this, it is fundamental to improve generalization capabilities in order to preserve performance in front of content created by unknown and new-emerging generation methods. This paper investigates in this direction proposing the idea to select a reduced set of CLIP-based features by resorting to a sparse autoencoder (SAE); such a set should ideally be termed the features of fake and could provide an improved generalization capacity. Experimental results carried out on extended datasets highlight this notable behavior and diversely designed detectors, based on this approach, achieve state-of-the-art performances.

title: R2H: Rowhammer attack in presence of randomized LLC
authors: Gaurav Gaurav, Prathamesh Nitin Tanksale, Venkata Kalyan Tavva
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: To defend against conflict-based attacks at the Last Level Cache (LLC), randomization is proposed. At the DRAM, to induce the Rowhammer attack, attackers generate memory requests that bypass the on-chip caches and hammer selective DRAM rows. The impact of the set-eviction based Rowhammer attack in the presence of randomized LLC is unexplored till date, and we find it to be ineffective. We propose R2H, a Rowhammer attack that works even with randomized LLC designs. By targeting a subset of cache sets, R2H drives the LLC miss rate comparable to that of conventional caches, in addition these misses are concentrated to specific DRAM rows, enabling a successful Rowhammer attack despite randomized set mappings.

title: Finger Vein Spoof GANs: Are they really useful to enhance PAD training?
authors: Tessnim Boulfoul, Valentin Pröpster, Andreas Vorderleitner, Andreas Uhl
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Information Forensics and Security (WIFS)
abstract: Four traditional GAN-based I2I translation techniques have been employed for the synthesis of biometric finger vein presentation attack instrument (PAI) samples (three public presentation attack datasets have been considered). These synthetic samples have been used to train presentation attack detectors (PAD). This work considers the more realistic setting to augment PAD training sets with synthetic data, instead of entirely replacing real by synthetic samples, as done in earlier work. Our analysis reveals that uninformed usage of synthetic data in the considered context has to be avoided as it can lead to dramatically high errors when trying to detect attack samples (high APCER). Instead, we recommend to use an augmentation of training data with synthetic samples only in case of too many false positive PA alarms (high BPCER values) when training with real data alone.

title:
CLAIR
                    <sub>A</sub>
                    : Leveraging Large Language Models to Judge Audio Captions
authors: Tsung-Han Wu, Joseph E. Gonzalez, Trevor Darrell, David M. Chan
publish: 2025 IEEE Automatic Speech Recognition and Understanding Workshop (ASRU)
abstract: Automated Audio Captioning (AAC) aims to generate natural language descriptions of audio. Evaluating these machine-generated captions is a complex task, demanding an understanding of audio-scenes, sound-object recognition, temporal coherence, and environmental context. While existing methods focus on a subset of such capabilities, they often fail to provide a comprehensive score aligning with human judgment. Here, we introduce CLAIR<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">A</inf>, a simple and flexible approach that uses large language models (lLMs) in a zero-shot manner to produce a “semantic distance” score for captions. In our experiments, ${CLAIR}_{A}$ more closely matches human ratings than other metrics, outperforming the domain-specific FENSE metric by 5.8% and surpassing the best general-purpose measure by up to 11% on the Clotho-Eval dataset. Moreover, CLAIR<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">A</inf> allows the LLM to explain its scoring, with these explanations rated up to 30% better by human evaluators than those from baseline methods. The code for ${CLAIR}_{A}$ is made publicly available at https://github.com/DavidMChan/clair-a.

title: Finite-element methods for noncollinear magnetism and spin-orbit coupling in real-space pseudopotential density functional theory
authors: Nikhil Kodali, Phani Motamarri
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: Spin-orbit coupling is a fundamental and complex relativistic effect that plays a crucial role in a wide range of experimental phenomena, including magnetic anisotropy, phosphorescence, and spin-orbit torque. This effect is important in various active research areas, such as spintronics, low-dimensional materials, topological insulators, and materials for quantum computing. The utilization of pseudopotential DFT has been shown to be effective in predicting various material properties when extended to account for non-collinear magnetism and spin-orbit coupling. We have developed a real-space framework and an efficient, scalable finite-element (FE) based methodology for non-collinear magnetism with spin-orbit coupling using ONCV (Optimised Norm Conserving Vanderbilt) pseudopotentials [1] , tailored for both multinode CPU and GPU architectures. To this end, we also propose a reformulated Chebyshev filtered subspace iteration solver for the solution of the resulting FE-discretized large-scale Kohn-Sham eigenproblem to capitalize on the enhanced single-precision computational capabilities available on modern supercomputing architectures. Furthermore, we have derived and implemented a generalized force approach for evaluating atomic forces and unit-cell stresses in a unified computational framework for geometry optimization involving non-collinear magnetism with spin-orbit coupling [2] .

title: Title Page i
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Virtual Reality and 3D User Interfaces (VR)

title: On the Difficulty of Token-Level Modeling of Dysfluency and Fluency Shaping Artifacts
authors: Kashaf Gulzar, Dominik Wagner, Sebastian P. Bayerl, Florian Hönig, Tobias Bocklet, Korbinian Riedhammer
publish: 2025 IEEE Automatic Speech Recognition and Understanding Workshop (ASRU)
abstract: Automatic transcription of stuttered speech remains a challenge, even for modern end-to-end (E2E) automatic speech recognition (ASR) frameworks. Dysfluencies and fluency-shaping artifacts are often overlooked, resulting in non-verbatim transcriptions with limited clinical and research value. We propose a parameter-efficient adaptation method to decode dysfluencies and fluency modifications as special tokens within transcriptions, evaluated on simulated (LibriStutter, English) and natural (KSoF, German) stuttered speech datasets. To mitigate ASR performance disparities and bias towards English, we introduce a multi-step fine-tuning strategy with language-adaptive pretraining. Tokenization analysis further highlights the tokenizer’s English-centric bias, which poses challenges for improving performance on German data. Our findings demonstrate the effectiveness of lightweight adaptation techniques for dysfluency-aware ASR while exposing key limitations in multilingual E2E systems.

title: A correlation-permutation approach for speech-music encoders model merging
authors: Fabian Ritter-Gutierrez, Yi-Cheng Lin, Jeremy H.M Wong, Hung-Yi Lee, Eng Siong Chng, Nancy F. Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE Automatic Speech Recognition and Understanding Workshop (ASRU)
abstract: Creating a unified speech and music model requires expensive pre-training. Model merging can instead create a unified audio model with minimal computational expense. However, direct merging is challenging when the models are not aligned in the weight space. Motivated by Git Re-Basin, we introduce a correlation-permutation approach that aligns a music encoder’s internal layers with a speech encoder. We extend previous work to the case of merging transformer layers. The method computes a permutation matrix that maximizes the model’s features-wise cross-correlations layer by layer, enabling effective fusion of these otherwise disjoint models. The merged model retains speech capabilities through this method while significantly enhancing music performance, achieving an improvement of 14.83 points in average score compared to the linear interpolation model merging. This work allows the creation of unified audio models from independently trained encoders.

title: A Novel Measurement Method for GIS Mechanical Fault Diagnosis via Physics-Informed Inversion of Feature Vectors and Attention-Enhanced Shallow Neural Networks
authors: Yuhang Li, Yadong Liu, Jin Bai, Dewei Ding, Yingjie Yan, Xu Hua, Xiuchen Jiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Gas-Insulated Switchgear (GIS) plays a critical role in high-voltage power systems, where structural defects such as bolt loosening or base dislocation can lead to catastrophic failures if not detected early. Traditional deep learning methods, including convolutional neural networks (CNNs), have achieved good performance in fault classification by extracting features from vibration spectrograms or time–frequency representations. However, these approaches lack physical interpretability, making it difficult to trace the causes of faults or generalize across different structural configurations. To address this limitation, a novel fault diagnosis framework that integrates Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) with an attention-enhanced shallow neural classifier (AESNN) is proposed, which first performs parameter inversion via PINNs to extract physically meaningful quantities. These quantities comprise equivalent damping, stiffness, strain energy, and the stress concentration factor, and are derived from limited vibration measurements. These parameters are then used as features to classify both fault types and severity levels in GIS systems. The results demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves superior accuracy when benchmarked against a portfolio of state-of-the-art deep learning paradigms. Moreover, the physics-informed approach provides exceptional fault classification performance across diverse operational and structural conditions, along with a high degree of physical interpretability lacking in conventional black-box models. Furthermore, ablation experiments validate the importance of multi-parameter coupling and the attention mechanism in improving classification performance. This study introduces a physically grounded alternative to black-box diagnosis models and offers a scalable methodology for intelligent diagnostics in complex electromechanical systems.

title: Point Cloud Perception in Human-to-Robot Handover based on Semantic Segmentation and Shape Completion
authors: Yifei Wang, Baoguo Xu, Linhu Wei, Weifeng Peng, Jiajin Wang, Aiguo Song
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Effective object handovers from humans-to-robots (H2R) in multi-scenario environments are a key challenge in human-robot collaboration. Numerous studies have explored various perception strategies. However, robust handover in complex settings remains challenging due to frequent lighting interference to RGB-based semantic segmentation, or inherent blindspot issue for limited perceptual views. Under these difficulties, this paper proposes a new perception framework for human-robot handover under eye-on-scene setting. Firstly, this framework uses a single-pixel RGB tracking for initial localization, a point cloud segmentation with regulated extraction, and a learning-based completion for camera blind spots. It works reliably encountering RGB overexposure and shows clear improvement in human handover postures at blindspot. Then, the method introduces a KNN-based loss to improve RandLA-Net segmentation, and an object feature classification strategy to enhance ODGNet-based completion. These boost recognition efficiency and generalization in real handover tasks. Finally, in handover experiments on 15 novel objects, our system achieved an overall success rate of 88.5%. In the quantitative evaluation involving four objects and five representative human handover postures, our method demonstrates improvements over the No Completion baseline in terms of both the overall success rate and the number of predicted valid grasp poses. When encountering RGB overexposure, our method exhibits a more significant advantage in perceptual robustness and grasping effectiveness compared to traditional RGB-based segmentation methods.

title: An Embedded Resolver-to-Digital Conversion Method Using Resonant Excitation and Quality-Driven Autotuning
authors: Ciro Attaianese, Gianluca Brando, Adolfo Dannier, Mattia Ribera, Ivan Spina
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Resolvers are widely used in safety-critical and harsh environments due to their robustness against temperature, vibration, and electromagnetic interference. Conventional resolver-to-digital converters (RDCs) often rely on monolithic integrated circuits (ICs) integrating excitation, demodulation, and tracking loops, which add cost and board footprint while limiting flexibility. This paper presents a fully embedded RDC that eliminates dedicated RDC ICs by exploiting PWM and ADC resources of modern microcontrollers. The method combines: (i) a GaN half-bridge resonant excitation engineered to align resolver sine/cosine peaks with ADC sample-and-hold instants; (ii) differential 16-bit ADC acquisition with hardware PWM–ADC synchronization at sub-10-ns resolution; and (iii) a software phase-locked loop (PLL) with quality-driven autotuning that minimizes the ripple of a monitoring function to optimize sampling timing, and performs automatic gain/offset calibration. Experiments on a 1.1-kW PMSM test bench comparing the proposed method with a commercial RDC-IC channel show comparable or superior position/speed accuracy (ripple < 0.5%), finer velocity quantization, robust tracking during acceleration, and significant reductions in cost and PCB area. The results indicate that excitation engineering and metric-based autotuning are effective enablers for next-generation embedded resolver interfaces in electric drives.

title: Bottleneck Transformer-Based Approach for Improved Automatic STOI Score Prediction
authors: Amartyaveer, Murali Kadambi, Chandra Mohan Sharma, Anupam Mandal, Prasanta Kumar Ghosh
publish: 2025 IEEE Automatic Speech Recognition and Understanding Workshop (ASRU)
abstract: In this study, we have presented a novel approach to predict the Short-Time Objective Intelligibility (STOI) metric using a bottleneck transformer architecture. Traditional methods for calculating STOI typically require clean reference speech. This limits their applicability in the real world. To address this, numerous deep learning-based non-intrusive speech quality assessment models have garnered significant interest. Many studies have achieved commendable performance, but there is room for further improvement. We propose the use of bottleneck transformer, convolution blocks for learning frame-level features, and a multi-head self-attention (MHSA) layer to aggregate the information. These components enable the transformer to focus on key aspects of the input data. The proposed model, in spite of having relatively fewer parameters, has shown higher correlation and lower mean squared error in STOI prediction for both seen and unseen scenarios compared to the state-of-the-art model using self-supervised learning (SSL) and spectral features as input.

title: A Self-Refining Framework for Enhancing ASR Using TTS-Synthesized Data
authors: Cheng-Kang Chou, Chan-Jan Hsu, Ho-Lam Chung, Liang-Hsuan Tseng, Hsi-Chun Cheng, Yu-Kuan Fu, Kuan Po Huang, Hung-Yi Lee
publish: 2025 IEEE Automatic Speech Recognition and Understanding Workshop (ASRU)
abstract: We propose a self-refining framework that enhances ASR performance with only unlabeled datasets. The process starts with an existing ASR model generating pseudo-labels on unannotated speech, which are then used to train a highfidelity text-to-speech (TTS) system. Then, synthesized speech text pairs are bootstrapped into the original ASR system, completing the closed-loop self-improvement cycle. We demonstrated the effectiveness of the framework on Taiwanese-Mandarin speech. Leveraging 6,000 hours of unlabeled speech, a moderate amount of text data, and synthetic content from the AI models, we adapt Whisper-large-v2 into a specialized model, Twister. Twister reduces error rates by up to $20 \%$ on Mandarin and $50 \%$ on Mandarin-English code-switching benchmarks compared to Whisper. Results highlight the framework as a compelling alternative to pseudo-labeling self-distillation approaches and provides a practical pathway for improving ASR performance in lowresource or domain-specific settings.

title: SLM-S2ST: A multimodal language model for direct speech-to-speech translation
authors: Yuxuan Hu, Haibin Wu, Ruchao Fan, Xiaofei Wang, Heng Lu, Yao Qian, Jinyu Li
publish: 2025 IEEE Automatic Speech Recognition and Understanding Workshop (ASRU)
abstract: Speech-aware language models (LMs) have demonstrated capabilities in understanding spoken language while generating text-based responses. However, enabling them to produce speech output efficiently and effectively remains a challenge. In this paper, we present SLM-S2ST, a multimodal LM for direct speech-to-speech translation (S2ST), built on the opensource Phi4-MM model. SLM-S2ST extends its predecessor by generating translated speech using an audio transformer head that predicts audio tokens with a delay relative to text tokens, followed by a streaming vocoder for waveform synthesis. Our experimental results on the CVSS-C dataset demonstrate SLMS2ST’s superior performance, significantly surpassing existing baseline models trained on the same dataset. Furthermore, when we scale up the training data and the model size, SLM-S2ST reaches on-par performance with the current SOTA model.

title: CRESS 2025 Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)

title: Quantitative Identification of Strong-Interaction Vehicles in Autonomous Driving
authors: Jinghua Wang, Guangquan Lu, Ailing Yang, Zhao Zhang, Miaomiao Liu, Jinhao Liang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Understanding vehicle-to-vehicle interactions is critical for safe and efficient autonomous driving in complex traffic scenarios. However, most existing behavior prediction and planning models implicitly account for interactions through soft weighting or metrics based on distance and risk, without a clear or quantitative definition of vehicle interaction. This paper proposes a metric called Vehicle Interaction Level (VIL) to quantify the degree to which a vehicle’s behavior is constrained by another vehicle. By applying a VIL threshold, strong-interaction vehicles are distinguished from weak ones. Then, the Feature Difference Level (FDL) is proposed to analyze feature-level behavioral differences across interaction categories, which is quantified using the Random Forest (RF) and the Gini Index (GI). Based on real-world vehicle trajectory pairs, an optimal VIL threshold of 0.2 is determined by maximizing FDL to identify strong-interaction vehicles, providing a more discriminative and broadly applicable interaction characterization than distance-based and risk-based metrics. Furthermore, the application of this strong-interaction vehicle identification method to trajectory prediction achieves consistently better performance than the baseline across multiple evaluation metrics, demonstrating its practical value for downstream autonomous driving tasks. Owing to its lightweight and model-agnostic formulation, the VIL-based method can be integrated into existing autonomous driving systems operating in highly interactive traffic scenarios.

title: A Proactive Triple-Mutation MTD Architecture Enhancing AI-Driven Intrusion Detection against Intelligent Adversaries in CIoT Networks_supp1-3679035.pdf
authors: Hao Hao
abstract: With the widespread deployment of intelligent consumer-electronics devices and the rapid expansion of consumer Internet-of-Things (CIoT) ecosystems that support next-generation intelligent systems—including smart homes, edge-AI gateways, and wearable networks—the volume and heterogeneity of device-generated network traffic have surged. To mitigate the limitations of traditional static AI-driven network intrusion detection systems (NIDS), proactive defence paradigms such as Network Moving Target Defence (NMTD) have been incorporated into device–edge–cloud architectures. Unfortunately, sophisticated adversaries may infer defence strategies and circumvent these mechanisms—leading to what we term the drug resistance of intelligent adversarial attacks. To counter this, we propose a reinforcement learning–based proactive Triple-Mutation Moving Target Defense (TM-MTD) framework that simultaneously mutates access proxies, IP addresses, and security strategies. The lightweight edge-level salience optimization ensures accurate detection in highly dynamic traffic environments, while the global policy differential optimization strengthens the defense system’s long-term resilience. Experimental results demonstrate that TM-MTD significantly enhances security performance against a variety of intelligent attack models, providing a robust defense framework for next-generation intelligent consumer-electronics systems.

title: A DC Voltage Robustness Control Strategy of Three-Phase PWM Rectifier Based on Active Disturbance Rejection Technology
authors: Gaoxiang Li, Pengpeng Huang, Xiao Liu
abstract: Under load disturbance, the DC-side voltage of the three-phase pulse-width modulated (PWM) rectifier is prone to fluctuation. To improve the robustness of the DC-side voltage, a single-loop voltage direct control strategy of the three-phase PWM rectifier is proposed based on the active disturbance rejection technology, which has no current loop. In addition, by building the transfer function model, the performance of the three-phase PWM rectifier under the linear disturbance and nonlinear disturbance are analyzed and compared. Compared with the voltage-current double-loop progressive control architecture, the proposed voltage single-loop control strategy can directly regulate the DC-side voltage with using the current information, which can enhance the dynamic performance of the three-phase PWM rectifier. Meanwhile, due to the application of active disturbance rejection control technology, the steady-state performance of the PWM rectifier is also significantly improved. Finally, experimental results verify the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed control strategy.

title: Learning to Pursue AC Optimal Power Flow Solutions with Feasibility Guarantees
authors: Damola Ajeyemi, Yiting Chen, Antonin Colot, Jorge Cortés, Emiliano Dall'Anese
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Control Systems
abstract: This paper focuses on an AC optimal power flow (OPF) problem for distribution feeders equipped with controllable distributed energy resources (DERs). We consider a solution method that is based on a continuous approximation of the projected gradient flow – referred to as the safe gradient flow – that incorporates voltage and current information obtained either through real-time measurements or power flow computations. These two setups enable both feedback-based online and offline implementations. The safe gradient flow involves the solution of convex quadratic programs (QPs). To enhance computational efficiency, we propose a novel framework that employs a neural network approximation of the optimal solution map of the QP. The resulting method has two key features: (a) it ensures that the DERs' setpoints are practically feasible, even for an online implementation or when an offline algorithm has an early termination; (b) it ensures convergence to a neighborhood of a strict local optimizer of the AC OPF. The proposed method is tested on a 93-node distribution system with realistic loads and renewable generation. The test shows that our method successfully regulates voltages within limits during periods with high renewable generation.

title: Dynamic Heterogeneous Regularized Physics-Informed Neural Network for Accurate MRI Breast Tumor Classification
authors: Jeba Malar M, N. Kanimozhi
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Breast cancer is the second most common cause of mortality for women, after lung cancer. Early identification of breast cancer can reduce the death rate for women. Due to the lengthy procedure of traditional breast cancer detection, an automated technique is important for early cancer detection. To overcome these challenges, this research proposes a novel Dynamic Heterogeneous Regularized Physics-Informed Neural Network with Duck Swarm Algorithm (DHRPINN-DSA) approach to improve the classification accuracy of breast tumors. Initially, MRI images are collected from the DCE-MRI dataset. Subsequently, the Adaptive Side Window Joint Bilateral Filter (ASWJBF) ensures excellent visual clarity by efficiently reducing noise while maintaining crucial structural boundaries. After that, Quantum K-Means Clustering (QKMC) technique encodes image features into quantum states and uses swap tests to compare similarity, which enables accurate clustering of questionable areas. Then, Clifford Fourier Mellin Transform (CFMT), which guarantees invariance to translation, rotation, and scaling, is used for feature extraction. Thereafter, extracted regions are passed through a Dynamic Heterogeneous Regularized PhysicsInformed Neural Network (DHRPINN) to enhance tumor classification precision. Finally, the Duck Swarm Optimization Algorithm (DSA) optimizes the DHRPINN weight parameter by mimicking the natural foraging behavior of ducks. This enhances model performance, and the fitness function increases efficiency and accuracy by lowering errors in classification. A model is proposed with an accuracy of 99.87 %, precision of 99.69 %, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score of 99.78 % which is able to classify breast tumors with high accuracy for medical diagnosis while minimizing the number of false positives.

title: Automatic Legal Text Summarization: A Survey on Techniques, Rhetorical Roles, Challenges and Future Scope
authors: Rubini A, Golda Dilip
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Text summarization is a branch of Natural Language Processing (NLP) that aims to automatically produce concise, coherent, and informative summaries from lengthy textual content. It contributes significantly in managing the exponential growth of digital information, especially in complex and domain-specific areas such as legal texts.
