title: Virtual City Traffic Simulator with AI-Driven Agents
authors: Jeba Malar M, Jaffron Shalom Kennedy R, Harish M
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: These testing environments are usually costly and time-consuming to deploy safely in real-world conditions. This paper describes the Virtual City Traffic Simulator with AIDriven Agents, an interactive 2D environment written in Python for modeling complex traffic dynamics. The developed simulator allows users to create a customizable urban layout design comprising roads, junctions, roundabouts, flyovers, and diversions, and visualizes how traffic flow would evolve in real time. Vehicle and pedestrian agents utilize a combination of rule-based behavior, pathfinding, and reinforcement learning to provide adaptive routing, congestion avoidance, and smart signal control. The overall architecture also provides mechanisms for the simulation of emergencies, accidents, and infrastructure modifications, enabling users to test various traffic management policies within a safe, virtual environment. Experimental evaluations show high accuracy regarding collision avoidance, congestion reduction, and stability in traffic flow on diverse scenarios. The proposed virtual testing facility offers a digital platform to researchers, planners, and policymakers for gaining insights about urban mobility and pretesting traffic interventions before physical deployment.

title: Fomo Based Brinjal Quality Assessment Using Edge Ai Model for Smart Agricultural Automation
authors: Jyothi M Patil, Meenaxi M Raikar, Channabasappa Muttal, Pooja B Patil
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Automated quality assessment of agricultural produce is required for reducing the post-harvest losses and enhance sorting efficiency. In the proposed system low-power and real-time defect detection system for brinjal (Solanum melongena L.) using an edge AI model deployed on a Raspberry Pi 5 platform is presented. The proposed model uses the FOMO (Faster Objects, More Objects) object detection framework optimized for embedded hardware. The model takes preprocessed RGB images of brinjals as input, extracts discriminative features through convolutional layers, and outputs predictions identifying defective and non-defective samples. The servo-based actuation mechanism allows automatic physical sorting based on inference results. The system achieves high accuracy with minimal latency, demonstrating its suitability for on-field and post-harvest sorting applications. Experimental results confirm that the proposed model offers an efficient, scalable, and energy-conscious solution for automated quality assessment in small-scale agricultural supply chains.

title: Human Pose Estimation for Intelligent Automation and Vision Systems
authors: Sneha Giridharan, S. Jeba Priya, D.Sujitha Juliet
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Human pose estimation has undergone significant advancements due to breakthroughs in deep learning, enhanced computational power, and innovative methodologies. This paper explores the latest techniques in pose estimation, particularly focusing on developments in 3D reconstruction, marker-less tracking, real-time processing, and applications across robotics, healthcare, and augmented reality. Recent advancements in technology have fueled progress in the HPE (Human Pose Estimation) domain. The advancement in the field has revolutionized machine perception; retrieving the pose of a person allows computers to make much more intelligent decisions, especially concerning human-machine interaction, understanding of behavioral intent, proxemics, and the state of the person, informing actions to be carried out by the intelligent machine. On review of notable approaches such as lightweight neural networks, hybrid frameworks, and ensemble methods are also discussed, highlighting their contributions to improving accuracy, robustness, and computational efficiency.

title: A Redundant-Channel Switching Method for Secure and Resilient Control of Industrial Systems under FDI Attacks
authors: Xinmei Li, Xuan Li, Xianghui Cao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: The deep integration of computation, communication, and control has driven the rapid evolution of industrial control systems (ICSs), improving automation, efficiency, and decision-making. However, this interconnectivity also exposes ICSs to cyber–physical vulnerabilities, among which false data injection (FDI) attacks are particularly threatening due to their stealth and ability to manipulate sensing or actuation signals without triggering conventional alarms. Such attacks can compromise state estimation and disrupt closed-loop control, leading to severe operational and safety risks. This paper investigates the problem of secure state estimation and resilient control for continuous-time ICSs subjected to coordinated FDI attacks on both sensors and actuators. To address this challenge, a redundant-channel switching framework is proposed to autonomously isolate compromised communication paths, while a generalized pairwise switching sliding mode observer (GP-SMO) is developed to simultaneously reconstruct attack signals and estimate system states. Using Lyapunov-based analysis, sufficient stability conditions are derived to ensure bounded estimation errors under bounded disturbances. Simulation results validate its capability to exclude corrupted channels, achieve accurate estimation, and enhance the overall resilience of industrial processes under sophisticated cyber threats.

title: ReGLeN: Resilient Critical Node detection in Cyber-Physical Systems via Self-Supervised Graph Representation Learning
authors: Yancheng Mu, Junjie Zhang, Lixin Ji, Shuxin Liu, Lan Wu, Qi Ouyang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: Ensuring the resilience of Cyber-Physical Systems against targeted attacks necessitates proactive protection of critical nodes, a task hampered by the reliance of existing methods on labeled data or incomplete structural metrics. This paper introduces a self-supervised graph representation learning framework ReGLeN for resilient critical node detection, which autonomously learns robust node embeddings by integrating network topology, node attributes, and semantic edge features derived from textual data using GPT-2. Key results show our method achieves a high AUC score for identifying critical nodes and has a lower ANC value compared with centrality-based and machine learning baselines. The derived node embeddings enhance classifier precision, demonstrating an ability to capture structural patterns inaccessible to traditional features. This work establishes a practical, unlabeled paradigm for critical node identification, directly contributing to enhanced protection strategies and improved resilience in complex CPS infrastructures.

title: Hybrid IoT-ML Framework for Metro Safety: Crowd Density Forecasting with Onboard Health Surveillance
authors: R. Surendar, T. Hariprasath, S. Karthick
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The way people commute by metro in towns poses a desperate situation with regards to safety of passengers during rush hours and emergency cases. In this paper, the authors introduce an IoT-based smart metro safety system, which integrates the real-time monitoring of the crowd density with passenger health by applying machine learning algorithms. The suggested system utilizes an embedded platform of Arduino Uno which is connected to various sensors such as heart rate monitors, blood pressure sensors, and temperature sensors to individual health tracking and the computer vision techniques to estimate the crowd density. Random Forest classifier was found to be 94 percent accurate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{R 2}=\text{0. 9 4}$</tex>, MAE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=\text{2. 8 3})$</tex> in forecasting patterns of crowd density in varying intervals of time. The comparative analysis of three ML algorithms, including Those that are Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Neural Networks, has shown that the former produces the best results, namely 79 percent accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score of the crowd classification tasks. A Neural Network model was able to achieve 66.7 percent accuracy in classifying alerts by classifying them into overcrowding, equipment failure, suspicious behavior and temperature deviations. The system has real-time data display by LCD displays, GSM-based alerts and UART communication protocols. Experimental outcomes prove the ability of the system to ensure the safety of passengers due to the provision of early warnings in case the crowd density is over the safe or critical parameters (90 people/unit area). It is a holistic solution because it provides metro operators with practical solution to proactive safety management and coordination of emergency response.

title:
The reliable preassigned-time consensus control of the second-order multi-agent systems: An event-triggered sliding-mode control approach
                    <sup>*</sup>
authors: Feida Song, Leimin Wang, Zhi-Wei Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: For the fast consensus control of unmanned systems, preventing the infinite gain of the controller is crucial for ensuring the safe operation of the controlled system. Firstly, by reasonably relaxing the convergence requirements of the system, a new practical preassigned-time (PPAT) stabilization theory is proposed. The significant advantage of this lemma is that it avoids the singularity problem that may arise in the design of preassigned-time controllers. Based on this lemma, a novel practical sliding-mode control (SMC) is designed and applied to the PPAT leader-following consensus control of second-order multiagent systems (MASs). Unlike traditional SMC, the design of this sliding surface does not require the sliding variable to be zero at all times for the stability of the system. The sliding variable only needs to converge to a small neighborhood around the ideal sliding manifold to ensure the system stability, which is more in line with the digital controller in networked environments. Furthermore, a corresponding event-triggered scheme (ETS) is designed to intelligently decide the triggering moments, reducing the communication frequency. Finally, some experiments are given to validate the safety and effectiveness of this algorithm in ensuring the rapid convergence of unmanned systems.

title: Graph-Theoretic and Deep Learning Hybrid Framework for Optimized Healthcare Diagnosis and Treatment Prediction
authors: Nirmala B, Sindhusaranya Balraj, Nongmaithem Aristotle Singh, Ramu Kuchipudi, A. Kaliappan, Vishal Ratansing Patil
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: In healthcare, where it is indispensable for diagnosis, treatment, and prediction, AI has sparked a revolution among many other industries. Medical imaging tests like X-rays, CT scans, and MRIs can be interpreted very accurately by AI-driven systems, particularly by deep learning algorithms, which can spot anomalies and disorders. By quickly analysing massive volumes of clinical data, these innovations allow doctors to make accurate diagnoses and educated treatment decisions. With an emphasis on data preprocessing, feature extraction, and classification, this study delves into the integration of AI approaches for healthcare diagnosis and therapy prediction. Conventional methods of feature extraction based on projections frequently remove unnecessary data but do not always provide the best results. To get over these restrictions, they offer a new graphtheoretic approach to object detection that can use detection sub-windows of any shape. A graph cut method finds the global best solution by combining local feature responses with boundary information, while the LLC approach finds the local feature replies. The suggested Graph-Theoretic LLC model achieved a 96.12% success rate in diagnostic prediction tasks, according to the experimental data. This shows that it has the ability to improve the accuracy of disease detection and provide more reliable healthcare treatment prediction.

title: Practical EM Side-Channel Security Evaluation of Low-Cost IoT Microcontrollers Using Raw Traces
authors: Ylicia Godinez, Jaya Dofe
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The increasing deployment of low-cost Internet of Things (IoT) devices in cyber-physical systems raises significant security concerns for embedded cryptographic implementations. While prior side-channel studies focus on lab-targeted platforms with dedicated measurement points, real-world, mass-market devices are less understood. This study systematically investigates the vulnerabilities of Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) to electromagnetic (EM) side-channel attacks on both XMEGA boards and Arduino Uno boards. Using correlation electromagnetic analysis (CEMA) and differential electromagnetic analysis (DEMA), we quantify attack success with metrics such as probability of guessing the key (PGE) and the differential coefficient. Our results show that while XMEGA platforms allow full key recovery with relatively few traces, attacks on Arduino Uno achieve only partial success, even after tens of thousands of traces, highlighting the effects of trace variability, environmental noise, and component differences. These findings provide a realistic assessment of EM attack feasibility on low-cost embedded devices and offer guidance for practical security evaluations beyond labtargeted boards.

title: Two Novel Constructions of Mutually Orthogonal Complementary Sets
authors: Yu Wang, Dejiao Cai, Yongqiu Chen, Zilong Liu, Chunlei Li, Xiuping Peng
publish: 2025 11th International Workshop on Signal Design and Its Applications in Communications (IWSDA)
abstract: This paper presents two new constructions of mutually orthogonal complementary sets (MOCSs), which have found wide applications in engineering. The first is a direct method utilizing extended generalized Boolean functions (EGBFs) to generate MOCSs with flexible, non-power-of-two lengths. This method can also produce complete complementary codes (CCCs) and is well-suited for hardware implementation. The second is an indirect method that combines existing complementary sequence sets (CSSs) and MOCSs to create larger sets. To further expand the existence space of MOCSs, we also propose a novel direct construction method for CSSs.

title: Real-Time Multimodal Anaemia Detection Using Deep Learning
authors: Snehal Rathi, Sneha Katole, Kunal Thakare, Soham Tawari, Rahul Solanke
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Anaemia continues to be a widespread health issue, especially in regions where basic diagnostic facilities are not readily available. Conventional screening depends on laboratory blood tests, which are accurate but not always feasible in field settings or community-level programs. In this work, we present a portable screening approach that evaluates anaemia without the need for invasive procedures. The system observes visual signs of pallor from images of the fingernails, conjunctiva, and palm while simultaneously recording physiological information such as blood-oxygen saturation and heart rate through inexpensive sensor modules. A compact convolutional model analyses subtle colour and texture variations in the images, and these visual cues are combined with sensor-given output to provide a more complete prediction. This multimodal design helps reduce the errors typically seen in image-only methods, particularly when lighting, skin tone, or device quality varies. Initial testing shows that integrating both visual and physiological signals leads to more reliable results, making the system suitable for rapid assessments in community health programs and other low-resource settings.

title: Towards AI-Mediated Bidirectional Communication Support for Cross-Neurotype Interactions
authors: Rukhshan Haroon

title: A 54.1–387.5- μ W 65-nm Multimodal SoC Integrating a Custom CISC Core for Edge IoT Applications
authors: Nan Wu, Shenyu Wang, Tian Tian, Zhihua Wang, Yanshu Guo, Hanjun Jiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: With the increasing demand for large-scale model deployment, cloud-based computing remains indispensable. However, energy and bandwidth overheads in edge-to-cloud data transmission pose significant challenges. This work presents a low-power, multimodal SoC that performs on-chip image and audio compression processing, enabling energy-efficient and low-latency cloud interaction for Internet of Things (IoT) applications. The SoC employs a two-level optimization strategy. At the microarchitecture level, a customized 16-bit fixed-length complex instruction set computing (CISC) microprocessor is introduced. Validated against a RISC-V minimal baseline RV32E equipped with equivalent peripheral engines, the proposed core achieves a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$5.06\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> reduction in core logic gate count and up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2.42\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> improvement in energy efficiency under iso-latency conditions. Additionally, a multipower domain strategy is adopted to further optimize energy efficiency, yielding <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$5.03\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$4.13\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> improvements for image and audio modes, respectively. Fabricated in a 65-nm CMOS process, our SoC integrates both image and audio processing, achieving an ultralow power consumption of 54.1–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$387.5~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>W. It outperforms state-of-the-art dual-modal processors, achieving a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2.84\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> reduction in image-processing core power and a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$12.78\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> reduction in audio compression power. Furthermore, our SoC achieves an energy efficiency of 206 pJ/bit of audio task and 15.5 pJ/pixel of image task, which offers a scalable and energy-efficient solution for versatile IoT deployments.

title: Efficient Densest Flow Queries in Transaction Flow Networks
authors: Jiaxin Jiang, Yunxiang Zhao, Lyu Xu, Byron Choi, Bingsheng He, Shixuan Sun, Jia Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Transaction flow networks are crucial in detecting illicit activities such as wash trading, credit card fraud, cashback arbitrage fraud, and money laundering. Our collaborator, Grab, a leader in digital payments in Southeast Asia, faces increasingly sophisticated fraud patterns in its transaction flow networks. In industry settings such as Grab's fraud detection pipeline, identifying fraudulent activities heavily relies on detecting dense flows within transaction networks. Motivated by this practical foundation, we propose the S-T densest flow (STDF) query. Given a transaction flow network G, a source set S, a sink set T, and a size threshold k, the query outputs subsets $S^{\prime}\subseteq S$ and $T^{\prime}\subseteq T$ such that the maximum flow from $S^{\prime}$ to $T^{\prime}$ is densest, with $\vert S^{\prime}\cup T^{\prime}\vert\geq k$. Recognizing the NP-hardness of the STDF query, we develop an efficient divide-and-conquer algorithm, $\mathsf{Conan}$. Driven by industry needs for scalable and efficient solutions, we introduce an approximate flow-peeling algorithm to optimize the performance of $\mathsf{Conan}$, enhancing its efficiency in processing large transaction networks. Our approach has been integrated into Grab's fraud detection scenario, resulting in significant improvements in identifying fraudulent activities. Experiments show that $\mathsf{Conan}$, outperforms baseline methods by up to three orders of magnitude in runtime and more effectively identifies the densest flows. We showcase $\mathsf{Conan}$'s applications in fraud detection on transaction flow networks from our industry partner, Grab, and on non-fungible tokens (NFTs).

title: Design and Validation of a Multimodal Immersive Virtual Environment for Cognitive Intervention in Patients with Alzheimer’s Disease
authors: Haixin Deng, Yujie Shang, Fengquan Zhang, Zhengxi Qian, Keng Chen, Huali Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Virtual Reality and 3D User Interfaces (VR)
abstract: Virtual reality (VR) environments have been widely applied in cognitive assessment and intervention research; however, most existing studies on cognitive load rely on fixed-difficulty paradigms that are insufficient for addressing Alzheimer’s disease (AD)-specific deficits, limit ecological validity, and risk cognitive overload and interpretive bias. To overcome these limitations, we proposed and validated a multimodal VR method with adaptive adjustment capabilities, grounded in the affective–cognitive impairments of AD patients. In this study, we further introduce a closed-loop adaptive VR method that dynamically modulates task parameters across trials in accordance with users’ real-time cognitive states. Eye-tracking and electroencephalography (EEG) modules are incorporated to enable synchronous acquisition and integration of multimodal physiological signals, supporting real-time cognitive load modeling and adaptive task modulation. The task battery combines emotion recognition and executive function evaluation with multi-level visual and auditory interference to effectively elicit AD-related cognitive deficits. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively maintains participants within an optimal cognitive load range, mitigating risks of overload and underload while significantly improving EEG signal reliability and behavioral validity. Our findings provide strong empirical evidence for the effectiveness of adaptive VR approaches in individualized cognitive assessment and intervention for AD, and lay a solid foundation for the broader application of immersive VR technology in neuropsychological research and practice.

title: Towards an Agentic AI Design across Edge and Cloud
authors: Shiva Sai Krishna Anand Tokal, Vaibhav Jha, Anand Eswaran, Praveen Jayachandran, Yogesh Simmhan
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: AI agents typically rely on massive LLMs which are associated with high resource demands and inference costs. This limits the deployment of complex agentic systems on edge devices with limited computational power. Furthermore, existing state-of-the-art agentic workflow patterns often struggle with long context management leading to excessive token usage and increased cost which becomes a problem particularly for smaller models with limited context windows. We define a novel agentic workflow pattern specifically designed for context window efficiency. It decomposes the task into stages to create opportunities for context optimization and efficient information retention across multiple steps of the application. This approach aims to significantly minimizes redundant context, enabling the execution of larger, multi-step agentic applications without exceeding the token limits of Small Language Models (SLMs).

title: PARISCV: A RISC-V Profiler for Application-Specific Hardware Optimization
authors: Spoorthi S, M. R. Ashuthosh, Bharadhwaj K, Vinay Reddy, Madhura Purnaprajna
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: The growing need for faster and more reliable computing has increased the use of application-specific architectures. RISC-V, with its simplicity and modularity, is an ideal platform for such design exploration. Application performance can be improved by adding ISA extensions, but while hardware implementations reduce execution cycles, they also increase area. Finding the right balance between software cycles and hardware cost is therefore crucial. PARISCV is a profiling tool that analyzes RISC-V execution traces to identify beneficial ISA extensions and potential custom instructions. It streamlines performance tuning by highlighting opportunities for acceleration. PARISCV’s extension recommendations achieved up to a 1.4× speed-up on the CV32E40X core with an area overhead of 1.15× and power overhead of 1.01×, while applications that did not require the B extension saw no improvement.

title: Vision-Text Interactive Hybrid Granularity Proxy Representation Learning for AI-Generated Image Detection
authors: Feng Ding, Yue Zhang, Mengyao Xiao, Kangkang Wei, Zhangyi Shen, Hong Rao
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Information Forensics and Security (WIFS)
abstract: With the significant success of Generative Adversarial Networks and Diffusion Models in visual synthesis, the risk of disinformation has also increased. Despite many researchers dedicated to solving this issue, they still rely on limited visual features when dealing with complex generated images. In this work, we propose vision-text interactive hybrid granularity proxy representation learning, which overcomes the limitations of previous methods that solely rely on visual information by incorporating cross-modal textual information. First, we design a cross-modal interactive reconstruction framework that uses a reconstruction mechanism to understand and learn information from different modalities, thereby preliminarily establishing semantic relationships between image-text pairs. Based on this, the hybrid granularity proxy representation learning method is introduced, which utilizes fine-grained proxy points within the same modality and coarse-grained proxy points across modalities to reduce the feature space distance between the two modalities. This method not only helps mitigate the interference of label noise on representation learning but also enhances the discrimination of the feature representations through text-guided cross-modal indirect alignment. We have carried out extensive experimental verification on multiple datasets, and the experimental results show that our method shows significant performance improvement in accuracy and generalization.

title: LCS-CTC: Leveraging Soft Alignments to Enhance Phonetic Transcription Robustness
authors: Zongli Ye, Jiachen Lian, Akshaj Gupta, Xuanru Zhou, Haodong Li, Krish Patel, Hwi Joo Park, Dingkun Zhou, Chenxu Guo, Shuhe Li, Sam Wang, Iris Zhou, Cheol Jun Cho, Zoe Ezzes, Jet M.J. Vonk, Brittany T. Morin, Rian Bogley, Lisa Wauters, Zachary A. Miller, Maria Luisa Gorno-Tempini, Gopala Anumanchipalli
publish: 2025 IEEE Automatic Speech Recognition and Understanding Workshop (ASRU)
abstract: Phonetic speech transcription is crucial for finegrained linguistic analysis and downstream speech applications. While Connectionist Temporal Classification (CTC) is a widely used approach for such tasks due to its efficiency, it often falls short in recognition performance, especially under unclear and nonfluent speech. In this work, we propose LCS-CTC, a two-stage framework for phoneme-level speech recognition that combines a similarity-aware local alignment algorithm with a constrained CTC training objective. By predicting fine-grained frame-phoneme cost matrices and applying a modified Longest Common Subsequence (LCS) algorithm, our method identifies high-confidence alignment zones which are used to constrain the CTC decoding path space, thereby reducing overfitting and improving generalization ability, which enables both robust recognition and text-free forced alignment. Experiments on both LibriSpeech and PPA demonstrate that LCS-CTC consistently outperforms vanilla CTC baselines, suggesting its potential to unify phoneme modeling across fluent and non-fluent speech.

title: Density–Hierarchical Clustering with PRI Transform for Robust Radar Signal Deinterleaving
authors: Isha Rajwar, Sathya Peri
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: We present a lightweight pipeline for radar signal deinterleaving that (i) clusters pulse descriptor words (PDWs) with HDBSCAN on frequency and pulse width, (ii) merges stable fragments via hierarchical agglomerative clustering (HAC) on four–dimensional centroids that include circular angles, and (iii) flags spurious clusters using a PRI Transform (PRIT) whose weighted spectrum emphasizes consistent pulse width and amplitude. The method yields clean emitter groups and automatically highlights multi-periodic, spurious aggregates. The approach is parameter-lean, template-free, and compatible with streaming.

title: SPLOCIS – Extending SPLOMs to a Scatterplot Cube with Interactable Shadows for Immersive Analysis in Virtual Reality
authors: Melanie Derksen, Viktoria Dieke, Torsten Kuhlen, Mario Botsch, Tim Weissker
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Virtual Reality and 3D User Interfaces (VR)
abstract: In data analysis, scatterplots serve as an initial tool for exploring the relationships between two or three attributes. While scatterplot matrices (SPLOMs) display every attribute combination through numerous 2D scatterplots to show a concise overview of a multivariate dataset, this approach is not directly suitable for 3D scatterplots due to visual clutter. Since research has shown that immersive virtual environments can enhance data analysis compared to traditional 2D desktop setups – especially for spatial analysis tasks – we propose an interactive system, called SPLOCIS, that makes use of virtual reality to enable users to interactively filter and select 3D scatter-plots from all possible attribute combinations. Our user study, combining both qualitative and quantitative results, demonstrates that SPLOCIS is a particularly novel and stimulating approach to work with multivariate data in immersive environments. It enables solving classic data exploration tasks in an efficient and accurate way, while not imposing unexpectedly high task loads. Moreover, our findings provide promising suggestions for further developments.

title: PointShopVR: Immersive Authoring of Large Point Clouds in Virtual Reality
authors: Tianfang Lin, Matthew McGinity, Stefan Gumhold
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Virtual Reality and 3D User Interfaces (VR)
abstract: This paper presents PointShopVR, an immersive point cloud authoring system in Virtual Reality (VR) that enables intuitive creation, manipulation, and refinement of large 3D point clouds. To support real-time interaction with dense data, our system integrates a simple acceleration data structure and a continuous level-of-detail (CLOD) rendering, ensuring high frame rates in VR. PointShopVR provides a handful of authoring operations—including point addition, deletion, labeling, copy-paste, translation and deformation—each offering instant visual feedback for seamless editing. We evaluate PointShopVR through a user study in which users complete diverse editing tasks within minutes, demonstrating both the usability and effectiveness of immersive point cloud authoring. Compared to traditional desktop-based tools, PointShopVR offers enhanced accessibility, natural interaction, and immersive feedback, making it a powerful platform for large-scale 3D data exploration and creative editing in VR.

title: Lightweight Prompt Biasing for Contextualized End-to-End ASR Systems
authors: Bo Ren, Yu Shi, Jinyu Li
publish: 2025 IEEE Automatic Speech Recognition and Understanding Workshop (ASRU)
abstract: End-to-End Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) has advanced significantly yet still struggles with rare and domain-specific entities. This paper introduces a simple yet efficient prompt-based biasing technique for contextualized ASR, enhancing recognition accuracy by leverage a unified multi-task learning framework. The approach comprises two key components: a prompt biasing model which is trained to determine when to focus on entities in prompt, and a entity filtering mechanism which efficiently filters out irrelevant entities. Our method significantly enhances ASR accuracy on entities, achieving a relative $30.7 \%$ and $18.0 \%$ reduction in Entity Word Error Rate compared to the baseline model with shallow fusion on in-house domain dataset with small and large entity lists, respectively. The primary advantage of this method lies in its efficiency and simplicity without any structure change, making it lightweight and highly efficient.

title: Robust Training of Singing Voice Synthesis Using Prior and Posterior Uncertainty
authors: Yiwen Zhao, Jiatong Shi, Yuxun Tang, William Chen, Shinji Watanabe
publish: 2025 IEEE Automatic Speech Recognition and Understanding Workshop (ASRU)
abstract: Singing voice synthesis (SVS) has seen remarkable advancements in recent years. However, compared to speech and general audio data, publicly available singing datasets remain limited. In practice, this data scarcity often leads to performance degradation in long-tail scenarios, such as imbalanced pitch distributions or rare singing styles. To mitigate these challenges, we propose uncertainty-based optimization to improve the training process of end-to-end SVS models. First, we introduce differentiable data augmentation in the adversarial training, which operates in a sample-wise manner to increase the prior uncertainty. Second, we incorporate a frame-level uncertainty prediction module that estimates the posterior uncertainty, enabling the model to allocate more learning capacity to low-confidence segments. Empirical results on the Opencpop and Ofuton-P, across Chinese and Japanese, demonstrate that our approach improves performance in various perspectives.

title: Quantitative Magneto-Acousto-Electric Computed Tomography (qMAE-CT): Imaging of Tissue Conductivity Distributions With High-Resolution
authors: Dingqian Deng, Yuchao Wu, Chenpeng Liu, Tong Sun, Mian Chen, Haoming Lin, Xin Chen
abstract: Magneto-acousto-electrical tomography (MAET) is a hybrid imaging technique that combines the ultrasound transmission with electrical detection to capture the conductivity information of soft tissue. However, most previous studies have focused mainly on reconstructing conductivity boundary information. To address this limitation, we propose a novel method termed Quantitative Magneto-Acousto-Electric Computed Tomography (qMAE-CT), which aims to achieve quantitative mapping of the full conductivity distribution. The proposed method delineates the forward process into three sequential steps, each addressed by a dedicated reconstruction strategy: model-based deconvolution, back-projection, and conductivity reconstruction using a Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN). The proposed method has been rigorously validated through numerical simulations, phantom experiments, and in vitro tissue studies. The results show that qMAE-CT can accurately reconstruct the conductivity distribution of irregular shaped targets, with a SSIM of 0.815, and the spatial resolution higher than 4 mm.

title: Special Issue on AI-Driven Optimization for Autonomous Vehicle Electronics [From the Guest Editors]
authors: Sousso Kelouwani, Bai Li, Basilio Lenzo, Vo-Duy Thanh

title: Llasa+: Free Lunch for Accelerated and Streaming Llama-Based Speech Synthesis
authors: Wenjie Tian, Xinfa Zhu, Hanke Xie, Zhen Ye, Wei Xue, Lei Xie
publish: 2025 IEEE Automatic Speech Recognition and Understanding Workshop (ASRU)
abstract: Recent progress in text-to-speech (TTS) has achieved impressive naturalness and flexibility, especially with the development of large language model (LLM)-based approaches. However, existing autoregressive (AR) structures and large-scale models, such as Llasa, still face significant challenges in inference latency and streaming synthesis. To deal with the limitations, we introduce Llasa+, an accelerated and streaming TTS model built on Llasa. Specifically, to accelerate the generation process, we introduce two plug-and-play Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) modules following the frozen backbone. These modules allow the model to predict multiple tokens in one AR step. Additionally, to mitigate potential error propagation caused by inaccurate MTP, we design a novel verification algorithm that leverages the frozen backbone to validate the generated tokens, thus allowing Llasa+ to achieve speedup without sacrificing generation quality. Furthermore, we design a causal decoder that enables streaming speech reconstruction from tokens. Extensive experiments show that Llasa+ achieves a $1.48 \times$ speedup without sacrificing generation quality, despite being trained only on LibriTTS. Moreover, the MTP-and-verification framework can be applied to accelerate any LLM-based model. All codes and models are publicly available at https://github.com/ASLP-lab/LLaSA_Plus.

title: Multimodal Zero-Shot Industrial Defect Segmentation via Visual-Textual-Geometry Fusion
authors: Dexing Shan, Yunzhou Zhang, Zhirui Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Industrial surface defect segmentation plays a vital role in quality assurance and production safety. However, industrial inspection systems often confront critical challenges, including scarce defect samples, multimodal data gaps, and poor generalization capabilities. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a multimodal zero-shot defect semantic segmentation framework. It leverages textual guidance as a semantic bridge to align and fuse RGB visual features with geometric features from normal maps, enabling accurate segmentation of previously unseen defect categories. Specifically, local surface normals are first estimated from the input point cloud using Principal Component Analysis (PCA), which are then projected and encoded into structured 2D normal maps. A text-guided cross-modal fusion module based on cross-attention employs a hierarchical, multi-path architecture to achieve deep semantic alignment among textual, visual, and geometric modalities, thereby significantly enhancing the model’s generalization capability in zero-shot scenarios. Furthermore, a prototype aggregation strategy is designed to reinforce texture representation in the multimodal feature space while suppressing background noise. Experimental results demonstrate that our method outperforms the latest zeroshot segmentation approaches on two industrial datasets. It also achieves performance comparable to state-of-the-art few-shot segmentation techniques, providing a robust and scalable solution for multimodal zero-shot industrial surface defect segmentation.

title: Interactive CAD Reverse Modeling via Variational Curve Approximation_supp1-3679473.pdf
authors: Yunfeng Ai
abstract: Reconstructing editable CAD models from mesh data remains a fundamental yet challenging problem in reverse engineering. Existing methods often struggle to achieve both geometric accuracy and topological consistency, especially when recovering analytic design intent from complex meshes. We present an interactive CAD reverse modeling framework based on variational curve approximation, which bridges mesh segmentation and parametric reconstruction in a unified manner. Our method extracts surface-aligned cutting curves from segmented regions and refines them through a variational approximation process that encodes primitive attributes and geometric constraints. This enables the automatic recovery of closed loops, constraint relations, and operation parameters, leading to a compact and topologically consistent CAD representation. In addition, a coplanar profile detection and voxel-similarity-based Boolean inference are developed to restore the topological order of the original modeling sequence. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach achieves higher geometric fidelity, better structural consistency, and improved automation compared with stateof- the-art techniques. The reconstructed models are watertight, topologically consistent, and readily applicable to downstream CAD design tasks.

title: Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor Stator Current AM–FM Model and Demodulation Analysis for Demagnetization Fault Diagnosis
authors: Jinduo Zhang, Haoran Sun, Tianyu Han, Xi Shi
abstract: The reliable operation of permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs) is critical in modern drive systems, while demagnetization of permanent magnets (PMs) remains one of the major faults threatening their performance and safety. Considering the coupled mechanical, magnetic, and electrical interactions, an analytical model of the stator current under demagnetization conditions is developed. The model reveals that demagnetization introduces complex amplitude modulation and frequency modulation (AM–FM) in the current signal, elucidating the underlying fault mechanism. The validity of the proposed model is verified through Fourier spectrum analysis, amplitude demodulation, and frequency demodulation. In the frequency demodulation process, the current signal is first denoised via solving the Laplace norm minimization problem and then adaptively decomposed using the direct fast iterative filtering (dFIF) algorithm to accurately extract individual instantaneous frequency (IF) components and identify FM characteristics. Finally, analysis of simulated and experimental signals validates the effectiveness of the proposed method, confirming that demodulation analysis notably enhances the reliability and sensitivity of the diagnosis.

title: A Single-Phase Three-Switch DC–AC Inverter with Wide Gain and Continuous Input-Output Current
authors: Hafiz Furqan Ahmed, Alireza Lahooti Eshkevari, Iman Abdoli, Mohsin Jamil
abstract: This paper presents a new single-phase three-switch DC-AC inverter with a built-in high-frequency voltage isolation transformer (HFT). Compared to the counterpart topologies, the proposed inverter uses just three switches and no diodes. This is the minimum number of switching devices reported for DC-AC inverters. This feature also reduces switching and conduction losses, resulting in higher efficiency. The proposed inverter offers both continuous input and output currents, which significantly improves both input and output power quality. The presence of HFT isolation not only meets grid connection requirements but also effectively suppresses the common-mode voltage and leakage current problems commonly found in traditional transformerless DC-AC inverters. This also allows for two degrees of freedom in adjusting the voltage gain: the turns ratio of the HFT and the duty ratio of the power switches. Additionally, the suggested topology is able to offer wide buck-boost voltage inversion, making it well-suited for connecting to renewable energy sources that experience significant voltage fluctuations. Detailed comparisons exhibit that the inverter achieves lower total switch voltage, lower total switch current, and switching device power (SDP) compared to various isolated inverters. To show the performance of this inverter, a 400VA experimental prototype has been designed and comprehensively evaluated.

title: Dynamic Data Scheduling and Precoding for Heterogeneous GEO-LEO Satellite Communication Networks
authors: Rong Chai, Xuemei Li, Jin Liu, Chengchao Liang, Qianbin Chen
abstract: Heterogeneous satellite communication systems consisting of geostationary Earth orbit (GEO) and low Earth orbit (LEO) satellites have attracted significant attention due to their complementary advantages in wide coverage and low-latency transmission. In this paper, we investigate the joint data scheduling and precoding problem in multi-antenna GEO-LEO heterogeneous satellite systems. Jointly considering the dynamic network topology, time-varying channel conditions, stochastic traffic arrivals, and queue dynamics, we model the problem as a long-term average cache queue length minimization problem under quality of service (QoS) constraints. To tackle the formulated mixed-integer non-convex optimization problem, we decompose it into two subproblems, i.e., data scheduling subproblem and precoding subproblem, and design a nested iterative solution framework. To address the data scheduling subproblem, we formulate it as a Markov decision process (MDP) and put forward a proximal policy optimization (PPO)-based data scheduling algorithm. Given the state and action of the MDP, the joint optimization problem is reduced to a precoding subproblem, which can then be transformed into a weighted mean square error minimization problem, and is efficiently solved via the Lagrangian dual method. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed algorithms.

title: Unveiling the Multifaceted Landscape of Biohacking: Insights from a Machine Learning-Driven Systematic Review
authors: Vito Santamato, Agostino Marengo, Lerina Aversano
abstract: Biohacking represents an emerging interdisciplinary phenomenon at the intersection of biotechnology, citizen science, and human enhancement, yet its complex landscape remains inadequately characterized. This study presents a systematic review methodology that combines PRISMA-guided study selection with a subsequent machine learning analytical pipeline to comprehensively analyze and classify biohacking's multifaceted dimensions. We developed an innovative multi-objective optimization framework combining FOX algorithm —specifically adapted for balanced topic extraction— and NSGA-II algorithms to ensure balanced topic extraction with optimal semantic quality and distributional equity. Through Latent Dirichlet Allocation enhanced by our optimization approach, we analyzed peer-reviewed articles from 2020-2025, revealing three distinct thematic domains: grassroots DIY experimentation, institutional health research, and biotechnological laboratory innovation. The study employed Gradient Boosting with four transversal themes—Ethics and Regulation, Social and Cultural Impacts, Sustainability, and Technology—as predictive features, demonstrating the feasibility of automated classification in this emerging field. LIME analysis revealed polarized contributions along a Social-Ethics continuum, with DIY approaches prioritizing community engagement while institutional practices emphasize regulatory compliance. Critical findings include the systematic absence of environmental sustainability considerations across all domains and fundamental tensions between community-driven innovation and institutional regulation. This research identifies biohacking as a complex ecosystem characterized by empirically identifiable organizational patterns, providing an evidence-based foundation for adaptive governance frameworks. The methodological-applicative contribution lies in demonstrating that machine learning-driven review enables objective quantification and reproducible analysis of emerging interdisciplinary fields—where the value resides not in scalability but in measurement precision and replicability—while the biohacking application generates substantive insights for evidence-based policy development. Our findings highlight the need for integrated approaches that balance technological innovation with ethical oversight, addressing the critical sustainability gap while managing the tension between democratized science and public safety.

title: A Multimodal Deep Reinforcement Learning Framework for Dynamic Pricing Optimisation in E-Commerce
authors: Yuchen Liu, Tianxiang Cui, Gangmin Li, Terry R. Payne, Yong Yue, Ka Lok Man
abstract: With the rapid expansion of e-commerce platforms, there is an increasing demand for pricing strategies that jointly optimise sales performance and profitability under highly competitive markets. To address this challenge, we propose a unified deep reinforcement learning (DRL) framework built upon ItaNet (Image, Text, and Attributes Fusion Network) for dynamic pricing in Amazon’s marketplace. By integrating multimodal product context into the DRL architecture, the framework enables pricing agents to reason over heterogeneous product characteristics and demand signals, leading to more adaptive pricing decisions. Extensive simulations across multiple DRL algorithms are conducted and compared against a range of strong baseline strategies. The results show that our approach achieves an average cumulative profit margin growth exceeding 16.7% aggregated across products and time steps, while incurring only a modest 0.3%–2% reduction in sales volume. This indicates a net improvement in total gross profit rather than gains driven by aggressive price undercutting. Overall, the findings demonstrate that ItaNet-based DRL policies deliver a more balanced and economically sustainable pricing strategy than traditional sales-driven approaches. The constructed dataset spans over 40 feature dimensions across 27 Amazon product categories, incorporating textual, visual, and numerical signals. Compared with commonly used review-centric datasets, this multimodal dataset better reflects real-world pricing environments and provides a strong foundation for future research on dynamic pricing and multimodal recommender systems.

title: Fuzzy-LFS: A Novel Localized Feature Selection with Fuzzy Region Division and Local Neighborhood Rough Set for Classification_supp1-3679190.pdf
authors: Yu Zhou
abstract: Localized feature selection (LFS) methods divide the whole sample space to determine region-specific subsets for classification which outperform traditional feature selection with a global feature subset for the entire sample space. However, existing LFS algorithms decompose the local regions by impurity level of samples, which can misplace critical samples and impair feature selection results. To tackle this issue, this paper proposes a novel Fuzzy Localized Feature Selection (Fuzzy-LFS) algorithm, which divides local regions around each sample based on Gaussian fuzzy membership to enhance the coherence of region-specific information. Besides, to handle data fuzziness and uncertainty, Fuzzy-LFS establishes a Local Neighborhood Rough Set Model with forward greedy optimization to search the feature subsets. A novel local classifier is subsequently developed, overcoming the limitations of global classifiers unsuited for LFS while mitigating the excessive dependency on impurity level in traditional local classifiers. Specifically, for high-dimensional datasets, we introduce a Localized Feature Relevance Pre-Selection strategy, assigning sample-specific feature subsets according to local relevance to assist in dividing local regions and enhance classification performance. Through comprehensive experiments on 11 low-dimensional and 13 high-dimensional datasets, Fuzzy-LFS achieves superior classification accuracy to state-of-the-art LFS methods, demonstrating its effectiveness.

title: Non-uniform Air Gaps Regulation Method for Hotspot Suppression of Ferrite Cores in 11.1 kW Inductive Power Transfer Systems
authors: Xian Zhang, Yaxin Li, Yize Wei, Fei Xu, Zhixin Chen
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: The local overheating problem of ferrite cores in magnetic coupling structures (MCS) of Inductive Power Transfer (IPT) systems has become a critical challenge limiting high power applications. In this paper, a non-uniform air gap regulation method between ferrite blocks is proposed to reduce hotspot temperatures on the ferrite core. The impacts of four air gap structures (lg1 - lg4) on the hotspot temperature of the ferrite core, system coupling coefficient, and leakage flux characteristics are systematically investigated. Subsequently, a multi-objective optimization design using the Non-Dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm II (NSGA-II) is conducted to determine an optimal combination of air gaps. To rigorously evaluate the effectiveness of the proposed approach, a comprehensive performance index considering temperature, efficiency, and leakage flux is constructed. To verify the feasibility of the proposed structure, an 11.1 kW/85 kHz experimental test platform is established. Experimental results demonstrate that compared to a uniform air gap of 0.4 mm with equivalent coupling, the optimized structure reduces the hotspot temperature by 8.5% and the thermal gradient by 27.4%. Consequently, the comprehensive performance index of the MCS is enhanced by 32.1%, confirming the method's effectiveness in improving thermal reliability without compromising electrical performance.

title: Lightweight Techniques for Federated Anomaly Detection in Log Data
authors: Anastasia Pustozerova, André García Gómez, Max Landauer, Markus Wurzenberger, Florian Skopik, Edgar Weippl, Rudolf Mayer, Andreas Ekelhart
abstract: Accurately and efficiently identifying anomalies within log data is crucial for maintaining the reliability, availability, and security of modern computing systems. In interconnected environments, log data often come from distributed sources such as Internet of Things (IoT) devices, industrial networks, or smart grids. Centralizing these logs for anomaly detection can be challenging due to strict confidentiality requirements, regulatory constraints, and the limited bandwidth of edge networks. Federated learning (FL) offers an alternative by enabling local model training on site, while aggregating only models instead of sensitive data, thereby preserving data confidentiality and reducing data transfer. This paper develops and evaluates a federated log-anomaly detection pipeline and analyzes its components. We adapt lightweight anomaly detection techniques in a federated setting, comparing them with deep learning (DL) methods, assessing detection capabilities, computational efficiency, memory requirements, and inference time. We explore the residual privacy risks in FL within the proposed pipeline, develop a threat model and suggest mitigation strategies. Our findings indicate that lightweight anomaly detection methods can match the effectiveness of DL techniques in the FL framework, while often providing improved computational and communication efficiency. Furthermore, these techniques show better resilience to non-IID data distributions, a typical FL challenge that can severely hinder the effectiveness of traditional machine learning models. However, the memory footprint of lightweight models varies with dataset characteristics and, in some cases, may exceed that of DL models.
